Title: Club Lunar (Full Festival Wristband)
Description: Club Lunar is a Fringe institution in the making and the new beating heart of the festival. After a busy day watching shows make your way to Flowersbrook and discover secret circus, a cocktail bar, bands, DJs and a late night cabaret every night. Each evening you'll find special guests headliners and an eclectic music policy spanning psych, reggae, swing, jazz, African, Latin and cumbia lovingly curated by the Ventnor Exchange team. Guest Headliners Fri 17th The SoulFire Project | Latin Cumbia Sat 18th The Turbans | Balkan | Global Fusion Sun 19th Evolfo | Brooklyn pysch Mon 20th Hula Hulagans | Hawaiian | Roots Tues 21st Fugo Kid | Hip-Hop| Jazz Weds 22nd Tommy Khosla & Jawari | Jazz | Sitar | Folk Thurs 23rd Sol’s ‘Hot Town Stompers' | New Orleans Jazz Fri 24th Tuba Libres | Brass Party Band Sat 25th Malphino | Cumbia | Tropical Sun 26th Ushti Baba | pan-European folk-rave Alongside these live bands will be late night cabaret, circus and the best DJ's of the Fringe providing the place to party!

Title: Marriage Material
Description: Charlie is on the hunt for a husband and needs your help. After enduring years of hilariously disastrous dates (cheers Hinge), true love has proven elusive so she is taking matters into her own hands. But is she marriage material? Journey with her as she blends comedy, poetry and theatre into this joyous antidote to breakups and singledom; laying out all the love, heartbreak, insecurity and joy of her past, before you ultimately have the deciding vote as to whether she is marriage material or not. Can you succeed where the dating apps have failed and help Charlie end her everlasting singledom? Or will she remain alone forever? Marriage Material is a 50-minute, one-woman show. It breaks down the boundaries of genre to connect with you on one of the most fundamental tasks within our society – finding our life partners.

Title: A Walk Through The Solar System
Description: Join Stokey Woodall, known as the Star-man, on an incredible solar system walk. This unique experience follows a 1,000-yard model, where each inch represents 100,000 miles in space Meet very day at 1400 hours, at the in Ventnor Exchange for a friendly celestial introduction chat .The walk will then commence from a scaled model of the Sun, conveniently located just meters away from the Exchange, outside the Mushi restaurant. and conclude at the downgraded planet Pluto at Ventnor Park

Title: Lights in the Desert
Description: Dear Someone, this is the last of four sets; the end of the globe. The theme for this final year is 'no one is an island, but I don't feel connected'. All original songs, played mainly on guitar. The music is just beyond the borders of country, blues, film soundtrack and primitivism. Inspired by western films, samurai films, cults, outsider art and road trip books. Next year you will be seeing other people.

Title: The King In Yellow
Description: Tide Nor Time Players present a live radio play performance of The King in Yellow, adapted and directed by Simon Bucher-Jones. This richly atmospheric production draws upon Robert W. Chambers’ haunting and enigmatic play, transforming it into an immersive theatrical experience that unfolds entirely through voice, sound, and imagination. Blurring the line between stage and mind! The performance invites audiences to step into a world where reality feels fragile and something ancient and unsettling presses just beyond the veil. The production captures the creeping dread and psychological unease at the heart of The King In Yellow. The infamous forbidden play, once whispered about in fragments now brought to life by Simon Bucher-Jones' original adaptation. Performed in the style of a classic live radio broadcast, the piece celebrates the power of storytelling in its purest form. Actors, sound effects, and music combine in real time to conjure vivid imagery without the need for elaborate sets, allowing each listener’s imagination to complete the picture in uniquely personal ways. This approach not only honours the traditions of early audio drama but also amplifies the unsettling ambiguity that defines The King in Yellow.

Title: UNDER MY SKIN
Description: A heart felt homage to the king of swing ,chairmen of the board and leader of the pack ..Mr Francis Albert Sinatra & friends... His life in songs and stories ,focusing on the untold truth of his mission to support diversity and inclusivity . Tales pictures and songs to celebrate an incredible life well lived performed by the souths premier storyteller and tribute with a wealth of theatre film and television credits , kick back in this cosy venue in the town centres 1st show and Act !

Title: Brainfools Circus + Theatre presents: Balance
Description: Two circus artists, two steel beer kegs, and a path of empty glass bottles. In Balance, Jared and Toffy find a physical language of trust in the inventory of a pub cellar. There are no traditional circus rigs—just the cold weight of metal, the sharp fragility of glass, and the constant negotiation of staying upright. They lift, they catch, they slip, and they hold on. It is an honest, high-stakes look at the visceral effort of relying on someone else to keep from falling. A circus duet about finding your centre when the ground feels like it's tipping.

Title: Frisky's Remix Roulette
Description: The Matriarch of Twisted Pop Cabaret takes you on a spontaneous and interactive genre-spanning extravaganza. Frisky and her incredible band bring you their five-star smash-hit show. And what will that entail you ask? Any song. Any style. You decide. Heavy-Metal Madonna, Opera WAP, DrumnBass Candle in the Wind. Come play Remix Roulette with us. 'Bold, brilliant, and utterly unmissable' * * * * * London Theatre Doc 'Genre-bending, audience-driven and laugh-out-loud funny' * * * * * The Reviews Hub 'Eccentric, hilarious, and outrageously entertaining' * * * * * Suzzi’s Spotlight

Title: Ruby Carr: BITE ME
Description: ​New silly stand up show from “enchanting human shaped hurricane” (Edinburgh Reviews) Ruby Carr. Ruby has been promised a Hollywood smile from her dentist in 32 weeks. The NHS only has 12 to fix her OCD. Working on her teeth and her brain all means one thing, there’s a lot going on in her head. The plan is simple: fix her teeth, fix her life, so that she can move up a metaphorical floor in her head and finally fix that pesky mental health. Nominated Best Show at the Leicester Comedy Festival 2026 “Ruby commands the stage with a radiant, infectious energy” (SLEEC REVIEWS)

Title: The Full English
Description: It's a bish-bosh, wish-wash, car crash mishmash!" From gangs of gung-ho Anglo-Saxons to Covidiots and Instagram influencers, this one-woman show by spoken word artist and linguistics nerd, Melanie Branton, celebrates the history of our glorious, multicultural tongue, through the media of performance poetry, silly hats and dad jokes. “A wonderfully eccentric, sharply intelligent, and surprisingly moving evening” ***** stars A Young(ish) Perspective “Funny, informative and clever” **** stars The Reviews Hub “Another triumph” Wolverhampton Express and Star

Title: Adam Flood: Life and Death of the Party
Description: Standup performed over big electronic beats. A show about Adam’s vanishing nightlife. ‘It shouldn’t work, but it absolutely does’, Metro. ‘A multifaceted comedy talent', Guardian. As seen on BBC and ITV. Adam, from Stoke-on-Trent, was once a teenage drug-dealer, then an indie rockstar, now a comedian. In 2025 his debut standup show was released by the BBC. In 2026 he ran, sober, full speed into a closed glass door. Broadway World ★★★★ Ed Fringe Review ★★★★ Stutter’s Pick of The Fringe ★★★★ The Real Chris Sparkle ★★★★★

Title: Pierre Novellie: Work In Progress
Description: Award-winning, five-star comedian Pierre Novellie swims over to try out his new show on you. As heard on Radio 4 and seen on television and, of course, the internet. “jumps from one ingenious routine to another...glorious” ★★★★ Dominic Maxwell, The Times “dazzling one-liners...gags so good that resistance is futile...a mini-masterpiece of extensio ad absurdum” ★★★★ Brian Logan, The Guardian

Title: Felicity Ward
Description: Nominee Best International Act 2025 NZ International Comedy Festival In 2025 Felicity Ward (Live at the Apollo,The Office - Australia) was the happiest she’s been in a decade. What happened? Divorce, Bisexuality, and Dancing with the Stars. And this bonafide showpony’s greatest gift is that she simply cannot shut tf up. 'great big snorting exhortations of comic laughter' ★★★★★ The List (UK) 'The strongest single hour of solid jokes comes from Felicity Ward… in a rolling, ballsy, crazy show that draws near-standing ovations.' ★★★★★ The Times (UK)

Description: Club Lunar is a Fringe institution in the making and the new beating heart of the festival. After a busy day watching shows make your way to Flowersbrook and discover secret circus, a cocktail bar, bands, DJs and a late-night cabaret every night. Each evening you'll find special guests headliners and an eclectic music policy spanning psych, reggae, swing, ska, jazz, African, Latin, calypso, rock-a-billy, and rock and roll, lovingly curated by the Ventnor Exchange team.

Title: Laugh Now Think Later
Description: Laugh Now, Think Later Three stand-up comedians bring sharp wit, clever one-liners and fresh observational humour to one punchy night of comedy. Tim Sque Opens the show — a rising act who reached the semi-finals of Hampshire Comedian of the Year 2024 and runner-up in Dorset Comedian of the Year 2024. Joe Fortis Follows with quick, subtly clever one-liners delivered with understated charm. Jack Clay Closes with sharp timing and likeable delivery, having performed at venues across the UK and toured supporting Shaparak Khorsandi, Chris Kent, Sean Collins and Jeff Dye. Laugh now. Think later.

Title: The Night Ali Died
Description: Ali is wanted by a vicious London Gangster and is placed under police surveillance for the protection of him and his family, yet one night, Ali decides to sneak out, sparking a bloody domino effect that ricochets through the sleepy streets of his city. Through the brutal accounts of Ali, a henchman, a detective and a mob boss, find out what on earth happened on the night Ali died. The Night Ali Died has been collecting a vast amount of accolades since, winning five awards in its first six months of touring. “A GRIPPING CRIME THRILLER THAT UNFOLDS WITH CINEMATIC PRECISION” – Colchester Gazet

Title: Dogmum
Description: Written by & starring Ventnor’s own Amy Ambrose. A musical rom-com for anyone whose Prince Charming turned out to be a cockapoo. When the dating pool resembles a crime documentary, one woman finds true love in fluffy dog Alfie. Funny, heartfelt & painfully relatable, DogMum is about heartbreak, healing and discovering that sometimes the happy ending arrives with muddy paws and terrible breath.

Title: Pick of the Fringe
Description: Returning for an unprecedented sixth year following completely sold out shows since 2021. Join us for a packed night of stand up comedy from a selection of some of the funniest acts at this year's Ventnor Fringe, curated and hosted by Edinburgh Comedy Award Winner and Ventnor Fringe favourite Ahir Shah (Live at the Apollo, Have I Got News For You, Mock The Week) Now featuring different line ups every evening so you can always expect something new, It's the perfect night out at the Fringe.

Title: Artist Breakfast hosted by Gallery 44
Description: Join us for an informal, social event with free pastries to meet fellow performers and creatives. Network, meet new people and discover more about development and support opportunities for artists in the region. Hosted by Crying Out Loud, who support and produce world-class, boundary-pushing visual performance work in the Solent region, UK and globally. Artist Breakfast events are open to creatives, artists and students of any discipline, whether or not you're performing at Ventnor Fringe. Meet at the Parkside Bar in the Fringe Village.

Title: Family Fringe
Description: Family Fringe is back for a third year running, curated by our friends at New Carnival, who have been delivering vibrant, exciting and innovative creative projects for over a decade. This area is specially curated for families looking for something fun for children, especially those aged 5-12, with everything from games to graffiti, carnival crafts and circus skills. Activities available include: Games – Hula hoops, limbo, hoopla etc, Graffiti and drawing Small world area Crafting activities Musical instruments Dress Up Box Glitter tattoos Circus Skills Dance Carnival costume making and large costume walkabouts! Circus Workshops with Azaria 14:00–15:00 each day! Entry to the Family Fringe is £10 (Including booking fee) per day per child (adults go free) There are also some spectacular FREE outdoor circus, magic and puppet shows in the park on both weekends, as well as live music on the Bandstand making for a packed, full day out. Please note children must always be supervised by an adult. Sometimes a particular activity may be busy, if this happens we will operate on a first come first served basis/ one in, one out.

Title: Why is the Sea Salty?
Description: A fun and dramatic reading of myths and legends for children and adults with a small jar of Ventnor Sea Salt to take home.

Title: Naughty or NeuroDiverse - An Aspies Road to Magic
Description: Bullied. Misunderstood. Unstoppable. From being expelled from three schools and surviving relentless bullying, to discovering a deck of cards that changed his life, Angus Baskerville’s extraordinary journey is anything but ordinary. Here he talks about his memoir 'Naughty or NeuroDiverse - An Aspies Road to Magic' & how he has harnessed his ADHD & Autism diagnosis to great effect, becoming a successful Magic Circle accredited magician along the way. He may even throw some magic in for good measure!

Description: Join Stokey Woodall, known as the Star-man, on an incredible solar system walk. This unique experience follows a 1,000-yard model, where each inch represents 100,000 miles in space Meet very day at 1400 hours, at the in Ventnor Exchange for a friendly celestial introduction chat .The walk will then commence from a scaled model of the Sun, conveniently located just meters away from the Exchange, outside the Mushi restaurant. and conclude at the downgraded planet Pluto at Ventnor Park

Title: SESKA - SUPERSILLYMAGICSHOW
Description: Super-hilarious family comedy magic for families. Crazier than finding a penguin in your fridge, one of the UK’s funniest family friendly comedians is back with a new bonkers show! Turbo-charged fast-paced super interactive magic show sells out at festivals and arts centres leaving audiences with a huge grin on their faces. Entertaining crowds from Mumbai to Trinidad and a firm favourite at Camp Bestival, his skills and jokes keep mum and dad glued to the show too. Endearing, charismatic, colorful and chaotic ! “One of the world’s top comedy magicians.” BBC Radio

The future of working life, today! We here at Corporate Management Solutions are delighted to invite you - yes, you - to attend one of our exclusive, behind-the-scenes presentations of the proper workplace of the not-too-distant future. Do you want the inside track to your success in the upcoming corporate utopia? Are you desperate to go above and beyond for a business that cares deeply about pretending to care about you? Perhaps a lazy, lollygagging loser wanting to turn your fortunes around? Or just in need of passing an hour in an exciting fashion? Whether you're any of the above or none at all, get your tickets ASAP for a uniquely entertaining and engaging experience you won't soon forget! Written and Performed by Tom Pagett, originally developed at the University of Chichester, before being redeveloped with the help of Lighthouse Poole's Sanctuary scheme.

Title: Canon Fodder
Description: CANON FODDER is a POETRY and MUSIC show, usually found at The Social and Glastonbury Festival, coming to Ventnor Fringe 2026 on a mission to feed the CANON and feed the SOUL. WRITERS including ELLA FREARS dazzle with their own work and that of poets they LOVE who've been OVERLOOKED by the CANON. LIVE MUSIC from the island’s finest musicians continues in the vein of NEW SONGS and UNSUNG HEROES. At the helm is HOST and HUMAN CANNONBALL, LILY BLACKSELL. Join us to celebrate POETRY and MUSIC, the HAPPENING and the HAPPENED! Raise a toast to NEW DISCOVERIES and those we LOVE THE MOST!

Title: The Mr Thing Show
Description: Mr. Thing are back "broadcasting" live at the Fringe. Become part of the studio audience as Tom and Owen, Puppet Steve and a crew of Things pull together a TV variety show live on stage with surprise guests, games, video sketches, live music, remote controlled cameras, musical robots, technical hijinx and much more. Welcome to the multimedia mayhem that is The Mr. Thing Show. 'A comedic riot' (Telegraph). 'I was howling with laughter' (Metro).

Title: ''Strange Young Alien" A conversation with Bid from the Monochrome Set with songs
Description: Join Bid, the singular voice behind The Monochrome Set, for a rare and intimate “in conversation” event, featuring acoustic performances of his songs. For over four decades, Bid has crafted lyrics that are witty, elusive, and quietly unsettling. If you’ve ever wondered what might inspire a line like “Every night at sleepy time / I hang my skin out on the line,” this is your chance to explore the answer—or at least venture deeper into the mystery. Drawing on his remarkable book Strange Young Alien, Bid reflects on a life shaped by travel, music, and unexpected transformation. Part memoir, the book traces his childhood in India and the Himalayas, his adventures within the British music industry, and the near-fatal aneurysm in 2010 that profoundly altered his brain—and his creative process. In its wake emerged what Bid calls “the Creature,” an artistic other self that challenges the very idea of authorship. Expect intriguing and often darkly amusing stories: the haircut that sparked “Letter from Viola,” the supermarket worker behind “The Cosmonaut,” and the unsettling figures linked to “Reach for Your Gun” and “Hello, Save Me.” Yet the more Bid reveals, the more his work resists simple explanation. Alongside these reflections, Bid will perform a selection of songs in a stripped-back acoustic setting, offering a fresh perspective on his distinctive catalogue—where sharp humour meets exotic imagery and a uniquely philosophical edge. Long admired by fellow musicians—famously including Johnny Marr and Morrissey—Bid remains one of the most original and enigmatic songwriters to emerge from the UK post-punk era. This is more than a conversation or a reading; it’s an invitation into a creative mind where meaning is fluid, inspiration is unpredictable, and the boundary between artist and art begins to blur.

Title: Huge Davies: Work in Progress
Description: Edinburgh Award Nominee Huge Davies (Cats Does Countdown, Off Menu) has not slept for a decade. To mark this ten year anniversary, he’s going to tell jokes about it whilst wearing a big keyboard; watch? ‘Ingenius’ Edfringe Review *****, ‘Masterful’ Chortle **** Telegraph **** The List *****

Title: The Guilty Feminist x The Road to Gilead Project - with Deborah Frances-White
Description: Join The Guilty Feminist and their bold new initiative, The Road to Gilead Project, for a live show that blends humour with radical hope. Since 2016, The Guilty Feminist podcast has racked up over 180 million downloads by examining the noble goals of 21st-century feminism and the hypocrisies and insecurities that undermine them. It’s the show that proves you don’t have to be perfect to be a force for meaningful change. The Road to Gilead Project aims to ignite connection and action that will bring about hope and resistance against the far right. Gilead is a pop culture shorthand from Margaret Atwood’s dystopian Handmaid’s Tale vision that we now see rising in the west. We welcome the questioning and the committed alike to come together in honest conversation about how we might form real coalitions for change and how we resist the slide towards opression and power abuse with courage, creativity and collective joy. Expect big laughs, bold ideas and a room full of people who believe a better world is possible - even if we sometimes feel like we’re winging it. Come join host Deborah Frances-White & her fellow comedians, activists and guests to be part of this joyful, defiant movement.

Title: Lawrence Dodd: This Can't Be It
Description: Perpetually derailed, deluded, and paralysed by overwhelm, Big Loz Dodd arrives to do something very special indeed - something that might just deliver the kind of profound insight that shakes our ‘contemporary situation’ to its core. Blending performance art and contemporary clowning, This Can’t Be It is a semi-improvised, feverish & open hearted exploration of how we stay open to the mystery when everything is closing us down. Directed by Lara Ricote and with Rachael Dobbie on tech.

Title: The Full English
Description: It's a bish-bosh, wish-wash, car crash mishmash!" Title: How I Went to Every Supermarket in the World
Description: Dan has been a data collector for 10 years. You know when they say how much it costs to live somewhere? He's the guy who finds out. He travels round the world and walks around supermarkets recording the prices of apples / cheese / you name it into his Dictaphone. He's been to pretty much every supermarket, in every country, in the world. This is the story of how. From the man who brought you How I Came Third in the North Korean Marathon. Praise for How I Came Third in the North Korean Marathon: “A great life-affirming tale” - The Dark Chat “Very entertaining and funny” - John Fleming

Title: Sam Nicoresti: Baby Doomer
Description: Winner of Best Show at the Edinburgh Comedy Awards 2025. A glistening diamond hour of stand-up from “delusional trans comedian” Sam Nicoresti about love, insanity, and the hunt for the perfect skirt suit. Following a critical maelstrom of success at the Edinburgh Fringe, and three weeks of sold-out performances at London’s Soho Theatre, Sam finally brings an extended and revamped version of her breakout hit show Baby Doomer to the great unwashed (i.e. goes on tour).

Title: Later Life Letter
Description: Later Life Letter tells the story of Luke Wright’s adoption – the life he leads, and the one he might have done. Wright navigates his audience through a warm and honest hour of poems and stand-up with the wit, pathos, and silliness that has made him one of the most popular live poets in England. Expect raucous laughter, tear-stained cheeks, and a little smattering of drum n bass. “A streetwise panache and a sardonic comic verve to rival Stewart Lee.” ★★★★★ Telegraph “His poems shoot arrows through the heart, or leave you dazzled by their virtuosity” ★★★★★ List Later Life Letter tells the story of Luke Wright’s adoption – the life he leads, and the one he might have done. Wright navigates his audience through a warm and honest hour of poems and stand-up with the wit, pathos, and silliness that has made him one of the most popular live poets in England. Title: River Man - Inspired by Nick Drake
Description: River Man is a 1 hour show inspired by the songs of Nick Drake and created by Jay River, a music student at Southampton University

Title: Cerys Bradley: On Road Signs (Work in Progress)
Description: STOP. Cerys Bradley (Actually Autistic Excellence Award 2022, as seen on Comedy Central, UKTV, BBC One Wales) has hyperfixated on road signs. Road signs are very pretty and they make Cerys’ brain go brrrrrrp. If you like road signs, follow the directions below for a silly and short history of road sign design as well as some heartfelt musings on the monotony of everyday life and the joys of autistic obsessions. GO. Outstanding Neurodiverse Performance Award Nominee 2025 ★★★★★ “Hilarious, absurd and utterly joyful” The Neurodiverse Review ★★★★★ “A very funny, quick-witted improviser” On The Mic

Title: Tales from the Isle of Wight Ghost Experience
Description: For over 30 years, Marc Tuckey has been enthralling visitors to and residents of the Isle of Wight with haunting historical tales of grisly ghosts, ghouls and ghastly goings-on. Many have enjoyed the walks he leads with his team – now is your chance to settle back in your seat for some more spooky stories – this time with a family feel. Whether you’re interested in the Island’s past; love a silly spectral tale; like a laugh or just enjoy a well-told story, you can expect a mix of little known facts from the past and some hilarious and haunting tales you won’t forget.

Perhaps a lazy, lollygagging loser wanting to turn your fortunes around? Or just in need of passing an hour in an exciting fashion? Whether you're any of the above or none at all, get your tickets ASAP for a uniquely entertaining and engaging experience you won't soon forget! Written and Performed by Tom Pagett, originally developed at the University of Chichester, before being redeveloped with the help of Lighthouse Poole's Sanctuary scheme.

Title: Canon Fodder
Description: CANON FODDER is a POETRY and MUSIC show, usually found at The Social and Glastonbury Festival, coming to Ventnor Fringe 2026 on a mission to feed the CANON and feed the SOUL. WRITERS including ELLA FREARS dazzle with their own work and that of poets they LOVE who've been OVERLOOKED by the CANON. LIVE MUSIC from the island’s finest musicians continues in the vein of NEW SONGS and UNSUNG HEROES. At the helm is HOST and HUMAN CANNONBALL, LILY BLACKSELL. Join us to celebrate POETRY and MUSIC, the HAPPENING and the HAPPENED! Raise a toast to NEW DISCOVERIES and those we LOVE THE MOST!

Title: Pirates Prov – Avast!
Description: Citizens beware - the dastardly crew of the vile sloop PiratesProv are coming ashore to Ventnor Town. Infamous for their improvised stories of adventure, swashbuckling and booty-hunting, these rambunctious rogues will stop at nothing to entertain. So come aboard the good ship Pirates Prov for barrels full of freebootin’ shenanigans. You’d be a scurvy ridden, toothless scallywag to miss out.

Title: The Mr Thing Show
Description: Mr. Thing are back "broadcasting" live at the Fringe. Become part of the studio audience as Tom and Owen, Puppet Steve and a crew of Things pull together a TV variety show live on stage with surprise guests, games, video sketches, live music, remote controlled cameras, musical robots, technical hijinx and much more. Welcome to the multimedia mayhem that is The Mr. Thing Show. 'A comedic riot' (Telegraph). 'I was howling with laughter' (Metro).

Title: Beyond The Title Presents...My Hero: Barry Cryer
Description: Beyond The Title Presents...My Hero: Barry Cryer

Title: Brainfools Circus + Theatre presents: Balance
Description: Two circus artists, two steel beer kegs, and a path of empty glass bottles. In Balance, Jared and Toffy find a physical language of trust in the inventory of a pub cellar. There are no traditional circus rigs—just the cold weight of metal, the sharp fragility of glass, and the constant negotiation of staying upright. They lift, they catch, they slip, and they hold on. It is an honest, high-stakes look at the visceral effort of relying on someone else to keep from falling. A circus duet about finding your centre when the ground feels like it's tipping.

Title: Li Jin Hao: Falling From A Moon
Description: I just can’t stop. Going into the past feels good. I never go into the future, I don’t wanna know. I want to know again what I knew. I don’t want to know the new. Ew. Edinburgh Comedy Award Best Newcomer Nominee and Chortle Best Newcomer winner Li Jin Hao is a comedy writer and performer. Li Jin Hao was made in China and marinated in Singapore, loved and feared for his dry wit, gritty softness, and surreal energy, he quickly became a BBC New Comedy Awards Finalist, and was accepted into the Pleasance Comedy Reserve 2023. ★★★★★ Telegraph ★★★★★ The Skinny ★★★★★ The Age

Title: Joe Wells and Abigoliah Schamaun present Neurodivergent Moments
Description: Two comedians, Abigoliah Schamaun (ADHD) and Joe Wells (autistic), read from their new book. “A fantastic introduction to what it’s like to be neurodivergent: funny; interesting; and free from the preconceptions and cliche that have built up around the subject.” - Frankie Boyle "Charming, moving, funny stories from the lives of two talented neurodivergent artists. Highly relatable if you’re neuro-atypical. Eye opening & empathy building for everyone else." - Deborah Frances-White

Title: The Full English
Description: It's a bish-bosh, wish-wash, car crash mishmash!" From gangs of gung-ho Anglo-Saxons to Covidiots and Instagram influencers, this one-woman show by spoken word artist and linguistics nerd, Melanie Branton, celebrates the history of our glorious, multicultural tongue, through the media of performance poetry, silly hats and dad jokes. “A wonderfully eccentric, sharply intelligent, and surprisingly moving evening” ***** stars A Young(ish) Perspective “Funny, informative and clever” **** stars The Reviews Hub “Another triumph” Wolverhampton Express and Star

Title: Katie Norris - Mother Succubus (Work in Progress)
Description: Boys of the manosphere: prepare to be spooked. Women with cats: your leader has returned. Last year’s Edinburgh Best Show Nominee is back and ready to enter a new era. A stand-up and musical comedy show about maturing adorably from the self-appointed Godmother to the Nation. As seen and heard on 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown (C4). Nominee: Edinburgh Comedy Award Best Show 2025 Nominee: Next Big Thing 2025 Winner: Best Show Leicester 2025 Winner: the Musical Comedy Awards 2023 'Oddball songs, surreal asides and a glorious persona' **** (Guardian) **** (Scotsman) **** (Standard)

Title: How I Went to Every Supermarket in the World
Description: Dan has been a data collector for 10 years. You know when they say how much it costs to live somewhere? He's the guy who finds out. He travels round the world and walks around supermarkets recording the prices of apples / cheese / you name it into his Dictaphone. He's been to pretty much every supermarket, in every country, in the world. This is the story of how. From the man who brought you How I Came Third in the North Korean Marathon. Praise for How I Came Third in the North Korean Marathon: “A great life-affirming tale” - The Dark Chat “Very entertaining and funny” - John Fleming

Title: Jim Thorn Quintet
Description: Jim Thorn (piano) is one of the Island’s best known jazz musicians and promoters, and organises the annual Isle of Wight Jazz Weekend which, in 2025 hosted a very popular latin jazz night to a full house, and Jim has therefore brought that lineup to Ventnor Fringe. Joining him are vocalist Loren Thorn, along with Joe Thompson (guitar and trombone), Jon Thorne (bass) and Ken Black (percussion). The quintet have all accompanied and played with a wide array of local and mainland-based top jazz names. In this format they perform instrumental and vocal (often in Portuguese) versions of latin and and other tunes, as well as a wide repertoire of the works of Jobim, along with plenty of bossa novas, sambas, original Brazilian compositions and some calypso-inspired tunes. The aims are good musicianship combined with a light-hearted and entertaining presentation. There are plenty of improvised solos and lots of latin energy.

Title: Unmasked - The magic of Angus Baskerville
Description: For his triumphant return to Ventnor after last years spectacular ‘Hidden Powers’, Angus Baskerville brings an all-new show ‘Unmasked’ that pushes his acclaimed storytelling and magic to new heights. Drawing on his own experiences of growing up with autism and ADHD, Angus finds new ways – and new tricks – to show us how we spend so much of our life hiding who we really are and how 'dropping the mask' can often make us feel better. Oh, and just for good measure – there’s a chance Angus knows where you’re going on holiday this year…

Title: Club Lunar
Description: Club Lunar is a Fringe institution in the making and the new beating heart of the festival. After a busy day watching shows make your way to Flowersbrook and discover secret circus, a cocktail bar, bands, DJs and a late-night cabaret every night. Each evening you'll find special guests headliners and an eclectic music policy spanning psych, reggae, swing, ska, jazz, African, Latin, calypso, rock-a-billy, and rock and roll, lovingly curated by the Ventnor Exchange team.

Title: Clownfish Out Of Water
Description: When clowns can't breathe water and fish can't breathe air- what's a poor Clown Fish to do in this cruel world? In 2025, Catling (Keep It Fringe Award Winner 2023, Malcolm Hardee Award Nominee 2023 & 2025) lost his slug mask. It was an essential part of his cult hit show ‘Moving on...really really slowly.’ This time, Stephen explores his life now that Autism has been “unmasked,” as well as his fin flapping rage against an increasingly soulless entertainment industry. "you’ll see things that you definitely won’t experience anywhere else in comedy." Chortle

Title: The Night Ali Died
Description: Ali is wanted by a vicious London Gangster and is placed under police surveillance for the protection of him and his family, yet one night, Ali decides to sneak out, sparking a bloody domino effect that ricochets through the sleepy streets of his city. Through the brutal accounts of Ali, a henchman, a detective and a mob boss, find out what on earth happened on the night Ali died. The Night Ali Died has been collecting a vast amount of accolades since, winning five awards in its first six months of touring. “A GRIPPING CRIME THRILLER THAT UNFOLDS WITH CINEMATIC PRECISION” – Colchester Gazet

Title: Around the World in Eighty Minutes
Description: The desire to express ourselves through music is shared across our planet. We tell our stories, give voice to our desires, recount our history and traditions, and look with hope for the future through songs and tunes that defy our differences and ignore borders. Singing songs or crafting soundscapes transports us to other times and distant places, that embody our cultural uniqueness and illuminate our diverse values and conventions. Around the World in Eighty Minutes brings you a taste of the extraordinary and exciting musical traditions, folklore, and customs from around the globe.

Title: Big Quiffy Bingo
Description: From the very towering tips of his perfectly-coiffed quiff down to the tips of his bejewelled toes, Boogaloo Stu is altogether completely ridiculous, simply fabulous and utterly idiotic. After another glorious sell-out season in 2025 at the Brighton Fringe Spiegelgardens and the UK tour that followed last summer, the legendary bawdy raconteur is once again taking his outrageous one-man show Big Quiffy Bingo out on the road. He is making a return to Ventnor Fringe for another night of gloriously playful and anarchic bingo, where utter filth, joyous fun and flashmob dances collide.

Title: A Walk Through The Solar System
Description: Join Stokey Woodall, known as the Star-man, on an incredible solar system walk. This unique experience follows a 1,000-yard model, where each inch represents 100,000 miles in space Meet very day at 1400 hours, at the in Ventnor Exchange for a friendly celestial introduction chat .The walk will then commence from a scaled model of the Sun, conveniently located just meters away from the Exchange, outside the Mushi restaurant. and conclude at the downgraded planet Pluto at Ventnor Park

Title: Club Lunar
Description: Club Lunar is a Fringe institution in the making and the new beating heart of the festival. After a busy day watching shows make your way to Flowersbrook and discover secret circus, a cocktail bar, bands, DJs and a late-night cabaret every night. Each evening you'll find special guests headliners and an eclectic music policy spanning psych, reggae, swing, ska, jazz, African, Latin, calypso, rock-a-billy, and rock and roll, lovingly curated by the Ventnor Exchange team.

Title: Scaravelli Yoga with Erling
Description: Yoga — but not as you know it. I'm back at Ventnor Fringe this year, and these 60-minute classes offer something quieter than most: a practice guided by breath and gravity, where you learn to move with your body rather than impose on it. Suitable for all levels.

Title: Fringe Frequencies: A Ventnor Sound Journey
Description: Step into a curated series of immersive Gong & Bowl Sound Baths inspired by the shifting atmosphere, coastline and natural rhythms of Ventnor. Each session carries its own distinct mood, frequencies and soundscape, connected through the creativity and energy Ventnor Fringe brings to the town each summer. Held beneath canvas in the park, expect resonant Sound, deep rest and the subtle backdrop of nature and festival life outside. Bring mats, pillows and blankets for comfort. Unsuitable for those with seizures, pacemakers or who are pregnant. Full details of contraindications on my website.

Title: Botanical Monotype Printmaking Workshop
Description: Come and create playful, experimental prints using fresh plant matter while learning about the monoprinting process. To make a monoprint, ink is applied to a plate with a brayer or a brush to create a layer of color. This inked surface can then be altered, added to, or masked with objects or stencils before being sent through the printing press. The result is a unique, freeform print that can be layered upon again and again! This workshop is for any experience level and ability—ages 12 and up.

Title: What We Do When We Can't Dance
Description: DreamArts Rep Company is a theatre company of young artists from refugee backgrounds, touring original productions fusing theatre, film, music and lyricism. What We Do When We Can’t Dance is a vibrant coming-of-age story packed with big laughs, bold beats and a shocking twist in a story of friendship, first love and finding your place. After finishing his exams, James leaves home for London with his unreliable older brother Jackie, leaving behind his first love Stella and best friend Moses. But on James’s 18th birthday, a revelation changes everything he knew about love, loyalty and the future

Title: A Walk Through The Solar System
Description: Join Stokey Woodall, known as the Star-man, on an incredible solar system walk. This unique experience follows a 1,000-yard model, where each inch represents 100,000 miles in space Meet very day at 1400 hours, at the in Ventnor Exchange for a friendly celestial introduction chat .The walk will then commence from a scaled model of the Sun, conveniently located just meters away from the Exchange, outside the Mushi restaurant. and conclude at the downgraded planet Pluto at Ventnor Park

Title: RONNIE FONTAINE: CLOSE-UP AND CRIMINAL
Description: Close- Up and Criminal plunges you into the East End underworld of the 1980s. Ronnie “Flash” Fontaine, a former London gangster, unleashes thrilling tales of bank heists, armed robbery, failed jobs, and narrow escapes. A master hustler, he used his magic to con, smuggle, steal, and slip away from danger. Close-up magic performed inches from your eyes blurs the line between myth and reality. Edge-of-your-seat storytelling, jaw-dropping tricks, and dangerous charm make this intimate experience utterly unpredictable - pure dynamite. One performer. Ten at a table. Thirty unforgettable minutes.

Title: Tetrapak Intaglio Printmaking workshop
Description: Come and join us for a special creative printmaking workshop led by Czech artist Petra Janouskova ART NOMAD on Tuesday 21st - Friday 24th, from 3 PM - 4:30PM. This workshop is for any experience level and ability—ages 12 and up. We will create a line-based design that will be transferred onto a Tetra Pak (a recycled milk or juice carton). The design will then be printed onto mixed-media paper using a printing press. This class will cover the full process of printmaking, from initial design through a finished print. Participants are encouraged to bring their own inspiration or choose from a variety of photographs provided by the class director.

Title: Whatever Happened To That Guy?
Description: In 2008, A-Lister Guy Wesley disappeared. Now, he’s writing his tell-all memoir. At least, that’s what failing author Bee Leigh wants her publishers to believe. Can she pull off the biggest hoax of the century* (*in the publishing world) before the yarn she’s spun unravels? Fresh off the back of three consecutive smash-hit Edinburgh Fringe runs, Maybe You Like It's brand new laugh-out-loud comedy promises fast-paced thrills, frantic multi-rolling and an awful lot of lying for a show inspired by a true story. Expect ‘a mountain of wit and meta-theatrical humour’ The Scotsman. Praise for previous work: **** 'Painfully funny' The Stage ***** 'exudes originality' The Recs ***** 'Simply the best show I've seen this year.' UK Theatre Web ***** 'Outstanding work' Theatre and Tonic 'spectacularly joyful and silly' The Independent Gems of the Fringe 2024"

Title: Funny That
Description: Three friends, each from vastly different backgrounds, Barnsley, Beckenham and Aldershot, come together to perform wry stories, wacky poems, and weird confessions. Do you like to explore the underbelly of life or just have your belly tickled. Do you enjoy having your assumptions about life challenged? Funny That bring together a rich seam of intoxicating life stories, humour & poetry.

Title: Mellow Drama at the Ventnor Fringe
Description: A long time ago in a galaxy not far away an evil Emperor develops plans for the ultimate weapon with enough power to subdue the whole known world with the assistance of a dark Lord. But he hasn't reckoned on the arrival of a rival whose destiny will bring new hope to those who refuse to go over to the dark side. Nor has he reckoned on the arrival of those who will join forces with this rival. Mellow Drama returns to the Ventnor Fringe to proudly present a short play in three Acts: “The Emperor strikes back” “Star Draws” and “The Return of the Magi”.

Title: Richard Pulsford: Yorkshire Dad, Immigrant Mum
Description: Multi-award-winning stand-up comedian Richard Pulsford takes a nostalgic no holds barred tour into his parents’ past to try to understand how it made them who they were, and therefore the person who he is. With a dad from Yorkshire and a ‘foreign’ mum maybe it shouldn’t be a surprise that Richard ended up being, well, ‘funny’. Richard won Leicester Comedy Festival’s UK Pun Championships in 2022, has had jokes in the Top Ten Jokes of the Edinburgh Fringe three times, was an interviewed guest on Channel 4’s ‘Steph’s Packed Lunch’ in 2022 and his one-liner show was nominated for ‘Best stand-up’ at Buxton Fringe in 2023. The run of his one-liner show ‘Get Rich Quick’ was a sell-out in the Ventnor Fringe in 2024.

Title: Burning Down the Horse
Description: The multi award-winning smash hit Burning Down the Horse comes to Ventnor! This immersive comedy epic drops you into the heart of the most iconic wooden animal in history - the Trojan Horse. Become part of this “infectiously funny” (Fringe Review) tale as you contend with heroic egos, class clashes and sword supply issues. There's only one rule: Odysseus' word is law. And for the love of gods - no naked flames! Created by the team behind last year’s smash It’s Gonna Blow!, join us and experience what really went on inside the belly of the beast.

Title: DELUGE
Description: A comic exploration of grief, inspired by a break-up and over 40 interviews gathered via a social media call-out. In the wake of a relationship, a woman tries to contain the overflow of her thoughts…She ends up stuck in a ladder, fighting against komodo dragons, house leaks, holes, and jam jars that only seem to multiply. Deluge puts a magic realist spin on dealing with grief, blurring imagination with real stories. ‘Intense physicality and irresistible charm’ **** (British Theatre Guide). ‘Incredibly beautifully written’ **** (Spy in The Stalls). ****½ (Youngish Perspective)

Title: Circus Trail
Description: Explore Ventnor's famous Botanic Garden after hours as The Circus Trail returns with an all new cast of acrobats and performers. Once again transforming these tropical gardens in a unique promenade performance, created especially for Ventnor Fringe. The perfect festival adventure for adults and children of all ages.

Title: POLICE COPS
Description: POLICE COPS debut show; a globally-acclaimed comedy blockbuster that has enjoyed THREE sell out runs at London’s Soho Theatre and an OFFICIAL SELL OUT run at The Edinburgh Fringe Festival. The 7 time award winning production has delighted audiences around the world at The Melbourne International Comedy Festival, The Cape Town Fringe Festival and The National Arts Festival of South Africa. PRESENT DAY (of 1976). Straight-laced rookie Police Cop Jimmy Johnson is out to avenge his brother’s death, and he’s got to go it alone! (with his partner) Teamed up with a disgraced, retired renegade named Harrison, the pair begin to unearth the soily secrets that the case holds. POLICE COPS is a cinematic joyride, speeding down Adventure Avenue in a souped-up squad car. Oh, and did I mention...the steering wheel is made out of guns.

Title: A RECKONING OF WOMEN'S VOICES
Description: A ducking-stool. A shrew’s fiddle. A scold’s bridle. Jean G-Owen’s troupe of Reckoners—Karl Whitmore & Sandy Kealty—fuse poetry, music and movement to re-stage the punishments designed to keep women in line, those scolds, witches, saints and dissenters who refused to say NO! ‘exhilarating, yet chilling’ ★★★★★ ‘a warning, a celebration, and a rallying cry.’ ★★★★★

Title: Taverner and James 'Rhyme and Reason'
Description: Bawdy bard Steve Taverner and opinionated newspaper columnist Cat James, return to Ventnor Fringe with a revised version of their 'Rhyme and Reason' show. Using poetry, prose, sketches and song, plus Cat's vintage school recorder, Taverner and James explore the issues which perplex, enrage and entertain us. With their opposing writing styles, can these two established social commentators find some common ground? Show contains strong language, adult themes, boomer dancing and references to nether regions.

Title: Richard Pulsford: Gold Fashioned Jokes
Description: One-liners and quality light-hearted jokes from the UK Pun Championships winner 2022, Scottish Comedian of the Year runner-up 2021 and You Must Be Joke King winner 2024. Nominated best stand-up at Buxton Fringe 2023. Top Ten Jokes Edinburgh Fringe 2019, 2021 & 2023. As seen on Channel 4's Steph's Packed Lunch. ‘Quite literally, just an hour of puns and one-liners… a breath of fresh air from some of the darker Fringe comedy shows’ (Broadway World) ‘Exudes confidence and charm’ ***** (Lancaster Guardian) 'An assured performer… quick, original and funny' **** (Broadway Baby)

Title: Whatever Happened To That Guy?
Description: In 2008, A-Lister Guy Wesley disappeared. Now, he’s writing his tell-all memoir. At least, that’s what failing author Bee Leigh wants her publishers to believe. Can she pull off the biggest hoax of the century* (*in the publishing world) before the yarn she’s spun unravels? Fresh off the back of three consecutive smash-hit Edinburgh Fringe runs, Maybe You Like It's brand new laugh-out-loud comedy promises fast-paced thrills, frantic multi-rolling and an awful lot of lying for a show inspired by a true story. Expect ‘a mountain of wit and meta-theatrical humour’ The Scotsman. Praise for previous work: **** 'Painfully funny' The Stage ***** 'exudes originality' The Recs ***** 'Simply the best show I've seen this year.' UK Theatre Web ***** 'Outstanding work' Theatre and Tonic 'spectacularly joyful and silly' The Independent Gems of the Fringe 2024"

Title: Webber!
Description: A bawdy, hilarious new musical about the life of composer Andrew Lloyd Webber (Jesus Christ Superstar, Evita, Starlight Express) as he's guided by Margaret Thatcher to achieve his dream of becoming 'filthy rich and f***ing useless'. Will he ever become a Lord? Will he fly back to London to vote to cut tax credits for the poor? Find out in Webber! 'Gleefully filthy!' The Scotsman 'It should run longer than Cats!' Speak The Speech 'An absolute hoot' British Theatre Guide 'Riotous!' Stage to Page Written/Composed by and starring Grant Sharkey and The Ventnor Darling's Laura Clare Reid.

Title: Club Lunar
Description: Club Lunar is a Fringe institution in the making and the new beating heart of the festival. After a busy day watching shows make your way to Flowersbrook and discover secret circus, a cocktail bar, bands, DJs and a late-night cabaret every night. Each evening you'll find special guests headliners and an eclectic music policy spanning psych, reggae, swing, ska, jazz, African, Latin, calypso, rock-a-billy, and rock and roll, lovingly curated by the Ventnor Exchange team.

A master hustler, he used his magic to con, smuggle, steal, and slip away from danger. Close-up magic performed inches from your eyes blurs the line between myth and reality. Edge-of-your-seat storytelling, jaw-dropping tricks, and dangerous charm make this intimate experience utterly unpredictable - pure dynamite. One performer. Ten at a table. Thirty unforgettable minutes.

Title: Smile Like You Mean It (final cut)
Description: Happy Jon has again tricked/manipulated/seduced/blackmailed the Ventnor fringe into letting perform new and old dark, random and sometimes perverse stories about life. (Free chewy sweet for all who attend)

Title: Final Queen
Description: A Drag Circus Horror Comedy. Priscilla Queen of the Desert meets Texas Chainsaw Massacre under the Big Top! Join Australia’s own circus queen Grace Lightning as she embarks on a road trip through the outback, dodging deadly snakes and spiders, bigoted cryptids, and chainsaw-wielding bushrangers on a murderous rampage. But are these monsters really what they seem…? Expect haunted acrobatics, blood soaked hula hooping, and a whole lot of hair-raising hilarity, oozing with all your favourite horror tropes on the small stage.

Title: RONNIE FONTAINE: CLOSE-UP AND CRIMINAL
Description: Close- Up and Criminal plunges you into the East End underworld of the 1980s. Ronnie “Flash” Fontaine, a former London gangster, unleashes thrilling tales of bank heists, armed robbery, failed jobs, and narrow escapes. A master hustler, he used his magic to con, smuggle, steal, and slip away from danger. Close-up magic performed inches from your eyes blurs the line between myth and reality. Edge-of-your-seat storytelling, jaw-dropping tricks, and dangerous charm make this intimate experience utterly unpredictable - pure dynamite. One performer. Ten at a table. Thirty unforgettable minutes.

Title: Ahir Shah: Golden (Work in Progress)
Description: HBO and Netflix star Ahir Shah (Live at the Apollo, Mock The Week, Have I Got News For You,QI) is back with his new stand-up show Golden. His last show, Ends, won the prestigious Edinburgh ComedyAward for Best Show, sold out shows across the world and was recorded as a comedy special for Netflix.Ahir has also been seen and heard on shows including Ahir Shah: Dots (HBO), Jurassic World 3, Foundation (Apple TV), Richard Osman’s House of Games (BBC2),The Last Leg (C4),The MashReport (BBC2),Frankie Boyle's New World Order (BBC2) and The Now Show (BBCR4).

Title: TBC (totally brilliant conjuring)
Description: British Magic Champion Mark Shortland will blow your mind with some totally brilliant conjuring. This fun packed show will engage the audience with a mixture of illusion, audience participation and a "unique brand of comedy" which are all blended together with an individual style of magic and suggestion, creating an act the edge of your seat was made for. "You've blown our minds!" Ant and Dec "Very impressive, one of my favourite acts" Jonathan Ross "Your performance was brilliant, so funny and full of life" Teller

Title: Scaravelli Yoga with Erling
Description: Yoga — but not as you know it. I'm back at Ventnor Fringe this year, and these 60-minute classes offer something quieter than most: a practice guided by breath and gravity, where you learn to move with your body rather than impose on it. Suitable for all levels.
