Each session carries its own distinct mood, frequencies and soundscape, connected through the creativity and energy Ventnor Fringe brings to the town each summer. Held beneath canvas in the park, expect resonant Sound, deep rest and the subtle backdrop of nature and festival life outside. Bring mats, pillows and blankets for comfort. Unsuitable for those with seizures, pacemakers or who are pregnant. Full details of contraindications on my website.

Title: Artist Brunch hosted by Creative Network
Description: Join us for an informal, social event with free pastries to meet fellow performers and creatives. Meet new people and discover more about development and support opportunities for artists in the region. Hosted by IW Creative Network, who support artists from across the Isle of Wight with networking, funding, training and development opportunities. Artist Breakfast events are open to creatives, artists and students of any discipline, whether or not you're performing at Ventnor Fringe. Meet at the Thirsty Shed Coffee shop at Ingrams Yard.

Title: Botanical Monotype Printmaking Workshop
Description: Come and create playful, experimental prints using fresh plant matter while learning about the monoprinting process. To make a monoprint, ink is applied to a plate with a brayer or a brush to create a layer of color. This inked surface can then be altered, added to, or masked with objects or stencils before being sent through the printing press. The result is a unique, freeform print that can be layered upon again and again! This workshop is for any experience level and ability—ages 12 and up.

Title: Norvil & Josephine: Rabbits Out of the Hat
Description: After their 5-star studded season at Edinburgh Fringe, vaudevillian duo Norvil & Josephine present a magical extravaganza for all ages! Together they are a magician with a secret and an assistant with a mission in this whimsical comedy musical magic show, the likes of which the world has never seen. It's 1905 and life will never be the same again. It's time for the magician to chase new dreams while the assistant calls the shots. Expect breathtaking magic, sensational circus and showstopper songs as these Magic Circle magicians share a spectacularly silly and spellbinding story about being yourself no matter what. It's a heartwarming story within a whacky feel-good show. RABBITS OUT OF THE HAT appeals to both adult and family audiences in equal measure. The show has appeared in 85 theatre performances around the UK including 4 sold out seasons at The Well Walk Theatre in Hampstead and a run at The Pleasance King Dome at Edinburgh Festival Fringe.

Title: SILENT DISCO STRUT: A Wild Moving Disco Adventure Through Ventnor
Description: Silent Disco Strut. Wild, ridiculous and fun. A flamboyant, entertaining moving silent disco adventure through the fringe and Ventnor. It’s open to everyone! It’s open air and hysterical! Grab your headphones and hit the streets for a joy-fuelled, interactive silent disco like no other. Strut, dance and sing your way through Ventnor in a gloriously ridiculous roaming party led by a flamboyant host. Expect spontaneous dance-offs, shameless singalongs and plenty of playful mischief as you let go, unleash your inner child and become part of the spectacle."

Title: A Walk Through The Solar System
Description: Join Stokey Woodall, known as the Star-man, on an incredible solar system walk. This unique experience follows a 1,000-yard model, where each inch represents 100,000 miles in space Meet very day at 1400 hours, at the in Ventnor Exchange for a friendly celestial introduction chat .The walk will then commence from a scaled model of the Sun, conveniently located just meters away from the Exchange, outside the Mushi restaurant. and conclude at the downgraded planet Pluto at Ventnor Park

Title: It Just So Happened - an alternative history show
Description: Award-winning stand-up comedian Richard Pulsford hosts this show for history fans. Three guest comedians / history buffs perform short stand-up sets based ‘on this day’ in history before exploring some possible alternative histories of Ventnor people and events. Recorded for the It Just So Happened history podcast. Previous guests have included Arthur Smith, Iszi Lawrence, Simon Munnery and Alasdair Beckett-King. ‘A fascinating and funny history podcast’ (Edinburgh Reviews, Edinburgh Fringe) ‘A quirky history podcast with a friendly splash of humour’ (The Reviews Hub, Brighton Fringe)

Title: A Haunted House
Description: Mime, clown and slapstick performer David Hoskin ('Phenomenal' ***** BroadwayWorld.com) presents A Haunted House; on a tour of a spooky old mansion, silly vignettes and strange time loops reveal murder, memory loss, and dancing zombies. It’s like spending the night with the League of Gentlemen and Jacques Tati in a Hammer horror film. In a dark, dark house on a dark, dark street, a house of horrors wakes from sleep... dare you enter? Mumbai Fringe '26 - Best International Comedy Show. ★★★★½ – EVERYTHING I LOVE about Fringe – Fringesider ★★★★ – Masterclass in physical theatre – BroadwayBaby

Title: RONNIE FONTAINE: CLOSE-UP AND CRIMINAL
Description: Close- Up and Criminal plunges you into the East End underworld of the 1980s. Ronnie “Flash” Fontaine, a former London gangster, unleashes thrilling tales of bank heists, armed robbery, failed jobs, and narrow escapes. A master hustler, he used his magic to con, smuggle, steal, and slip away from danger. Close-up magic performed inches from your eyes blurs the line between myth and reality. Edge-of-your-seat storytelling, jaw-dropping tricks, and dangerous charm make this intimate experience utterly unpredictable - pure dynamite. One performer. Ten at a table. Thirty unforgettable minutes.

Title: SILENT DISCO STRUT: A Wild Moving Disco Adventure Through Ventnor
Description: Silent Disco Strut. Wild, ridiculous and fun. A flamboyant, entertaining moving silent disco adventure through the fringe and Ventnor. It’s open to everyone! It’s open air and hysterical! Grab your headphones and hit the streets for a joy-fuelled, interactive silent disco like no other. Strut, dance and sing your way through Ventnor in a gloriously ridiculous roaming party led by a flamboyant host. Expect spontaneous dance-offs, shameless singalongs and plenty of playful mischief as you let go, unleash your inner child and become part of the spectacle."

Title: Tetrapak Intaglio Printmaking workshop
Description: Come and join us for a special creative printmaking workshop led by Czech artist Petra Janouskova ART NOMAD on Tuesday 21st - Friday 24th, from 3 PM - 4:30PM. This workshop is for any experience level and ability—ages 12 and up. We will create a line-based design that will be transferred onto a Tetra Pak (a recycled milk or juice carton). The design will then be printed onto mixed-media paper using a printing press. This class will cover the full process of printmaking, from initial design through a finished print. Participants are encouraged to bring their own inspiration or choose from a variety of photographs provided by the class director.

Title: Mellow Drama at the Ventnor Fringe
Description: A long time ago in a galaxy not far away an evil Emperor develops plans for the ultimate weapon with enough power to subdue the whole known world with the assistance of a dark Lord. But he hasn't reckoned on the arrival of a rival whose destiny will bring new hope to those who refuse to go over to the dark side. Nor has he reckoned on the arrival of those who will join forces with this rival. Mellow Drama returns to the Ventnor Fringe to proudly present a short play in three Acts: “The Emperor strikes back” “Star Draws” and “The Return of the Magi”.

Title: Monty Storyteller — Solent Tales, Macabre Stories
Description: It's a time for spying, and a time for sabotage. The last quarter of the 18th century and Britain is at war with France and America. Portsmouth, as the home of the Navy, is a target... So come here tell, of John the Painter, incendiary and arsonist, hanged, on The Hard, from the highest gallows to be used in England. And of David Tyrie, whose particularly gruesome execution on Southsea Common pulled in a crowd to rival that of the Victorious Festival today. And of... well, that would be telling... Taking time out from tale telling alongside some of the island's finest musicians, traditional storyteller Tony "Monty" Hirst brings a solo show of historical tales to Ventnor Fringe. Carefully researched from contemporary newspaper sources of the time, which will be surely made available as storynotes merch, Monty has read them all and will tell you what he remembers. Because it's the story that's left when the facts have been forgotten. Find out more from: montystoryteller.org

Title: RONNIE FONTAINE: CLOSE-UP AND CRIMINAL
Description: Close- Up and Criminal plunges you into the East End underworld of the 1980s. Ronnie “Flash” Fontaine, a former London gangster, unleashes thrilling tales of bank heists, armed robbery, failed jobs, and narrow escapes. A master hustler, he used his magic to con, smuggle, steal, and slip away from danger. Close-up magic performed inches from your eyes blurs the line between myth and reality. Edge-of-your-seat storytelling, jaw-dropping tricks, and dangerous charm make this intimate experience utterly unpredictable - pure dynamite. One performer. Ten at a table. Thirty unforgettable minutes.

Title: Richard Pulsford: Yorkshire Dad, Immigrant Mum
Description: Multi-award-winning stand-up comedian Richard Pulsford takes a nostalgic no holds barred tour into his parents’ past to try to understand how it made them who they were, and therefore the person who he is. With a dad from Yorkshire and a ‘foreign’ mum maybe it shouldn’t be a surprise that Richard ended up being, well, ‘funny’. Richard won Leicester Comedy Festival’s UK Pun Championships in 2022, has had jokes in the Top Ten Jokes of the Edinburgh Fringe three times, was an interviewed guest on Channel 4’s ‘Steph’s Packed Lunch’ in 2022 and his one-liner show was nominated for ‘Best stand-up’ at Buxton Fringe in 2023. The run of his one-liner show ‘Get Rich Quick’ was a sell-out in the Ventnor Fringe in 2024.

Title: Burning Down the Horse
Description: The multi award-winning smash hit Burning Down the Horse comes to Ventnor! This immersive comedy epic drops you into the heart of the most iconic wooden animal in history - the Trojan Horse. Become part of this “infectiously funny” (Fringe Review) tale as you contend with heroic egos, class clashes and sword supply issues. There's only one rule: Odysseus' word is law. And for the love of gods - no naked flames! Created by the team behind last year’s smash It’s Gonna Blow!, join us and experience what really went on inside the belly of the beast.

Title: DELUGE
Description: A comic exploration of grief, inspired by a break-up and over 40 interviews gathered via a social media call-out. In the wake of a relationship, a woman tries to contain the overflow of her thoughts…She ends up stuck in a ladder, fighting against komodo dragons, house leaks, holes, and jam jars that only seem to multiply. Deluge puts a magic realist spin on dealing with grief, blurring imagination with real stories. ‘Intense physicality and irresistible charm’ **** (British Theatre Guide). ‘Incredibly beautifully written’ **** (Spy in The Stalls). ****½ (Youngish Perspective)

Title: Circus Trail
Description: Explore Ventnor's famous Botanic Garden after hours as The Circus Trail returns with an all new cast of acrobats and performers. Once again transforming these tropical gardens in a unique promenade performance, created especially for Ventnor Fringe. The perfect festival adventure for adults and children of all ages.

Title: POLICE COPS
Description: POLICE COPS debut show; a globally-acclaimed comedy blockbuster that has enjoyed THREE sell out runs at London’s Soho Theatre and an OFFICIAL SELL OUT run at The Edinburgh Fringe Festival. The 7 time award winning production has delighted audiences around the world at The Melbourne International Comedy Festival, The Cape Town Fringe Festival and The National Arts Festival of South Africa. PRESENT DAY (of 1976). Straight-laced rookie Police Cop Jimmy Johnson is out to avenge his brother’s death, and he’s got to go it alone! (with his partner) Teamed up with a disgraced, retired renegade named Harrison, the pair begin to unearth the soily secrets that the case holds. POLICE COPS is a cinematic joyride, speeding down Adventure Avenue in a souped-up squad car. Oh, and did I mention...the steering wheel is made out of guns.

Title: Molly McGuinness
Description: After a 5 star, award-nominated, totally sold out run at the Edinburgh Fringe, Molly McGuinness is going to be mixing her debut show Slob with some brand new material prepping for her next show. As seen on Comedy Central Live and heard on BBC Radio 4. BBC New Comedy Awards finalist. Tour support for Joe Lycett, Sophie Duker and Rachel Fairburn. “A supremely funny set from a future star” ***** The Herald “A formidable debut” **** Chortle “Audacity and Northern charm” **** Beyond The Joke

Title: Taverner and James 'Rhyme and Reason'
Description: Bawdy bard Steve Taverner and opinionated newspaper columnist Cat James, return to Ventnor Fringe with a revised version of their 'Rhyme and Reason' show. Using poetry, prose, sketches and song, plus Cat's vintage school recorder, Taverner and James explore the issues which perplex, enrage and entertain us. With their opposing writing styles, can these two established social commentators find some common ground? Show contains strong language, adult themes, boomer dancing and references to nether regions.

Title: Medieval Medley
Description: Step back in time for an evening of Medieval mischief, mystery and mayhem! These timeless tales feature a cast of truly unforgettable characters. A gang of youths embark on a pursuit to hunt down death, a clueless carpenter braces for a biblical flood and a swindler swears he has the skill of turning sticks into silver. So join the Island Storytellers for an evening of warmth, wisdom and wit in this fun and lively celebration of community storytelling.

Title: Richard Pulsford: Gold Fashioned Jokes
Description: One-liners and quality light-hearted jokes from the UK Pun Championships winner 2022, Scottish Comedian of the Year runner-up 2021 and You Must Be Joke King winner 2024. Nominated best stand-up at Buxton Fringe 2023. Top Ten Jokes Edinburgh Fringe 2019, 2021 & 2023. As seen on Channel 4's Steph's Packed Lunch. ‘Quite literally, just an hour of puns and one-liners… a breath of fresh air from some of the darker Fringe comedy shows’ (Broadway World) ‘Exudes confidence and charm’ ***** (Lancaster Guardian) 'An assured performer… quick, original and funny' **** (Broadway Baby)

Title: Whatever Happened To That Guy?
Description: In 2008, A-Lister Guy Wesley disappeared. Now, he’s writing his tell-all memoir. At least, that’s what failing author Bee Leigh wants her publishers to believe. Can she pull off the biggest hoax of the century* (*in the publishing world) before the yarn she’s spun unravels? Fresh off the back of three consecutive smash-hit Edinburgh Fringe runs, Maybe You Like It's brand new laugh-out-loud comedy promises fast-paced thrills, frantic multi-rolling and an awful lot of lying for a show inspired by a true story. Expect ‘a mountain of wit and meta-theatrical humour’ The Scotsman. Praise for previous work: **** 'Painfully funny' The Stage ***** 'exudes originality' The Recs ***** 'Simply the best show I've seen this year.' UK Theatre Web ***** 'Outstanding work' Theatre and Tonic 'spectacularly joyful and silly' The Independent Gems of the Fringe 2024"

Title: Andy Roberts Acoustic Island
Description: Andy Roberts presents an evening of brilliant Acoustic Island musicians over two hours with an interval. The cast includes emerging and established talents comprising duos and solos in a variety of acoustic styles from Singer Songwriter to Country, Classic Folk Blues and Modern Originals. Featuring: * Daysi and Amelia - harmony vocals and guitar duo from Ventnor itself. * Kindred Found - radio and festival Americana stars * Andy Roberts - Singer Songwriter, Playwright and tonight your MC. * Plus maybe more!

Title: Rob Auton - JUPITER
Description: I am writing a new show. It is a story about me being an estate agent on the planet Jupiter. It would be great to see you if you fancy it? 'A genuine original' The Guardian 'We need him' The Scotsman 'Just sad' Vanessa Feltz

Title: Club Lunar
Description: Club Lunar is a Fringe institution in the making and the new beating heart of the festival. After a busy day watching shows make your way to Flowersbrook and discover secret circus, a cocktail bar, bands, DJs and a late-night cabaret every night. Each evening you'll find special guests headliners and an eclectic music policy spanning psych, reggae, swing, ska, jazz, African, Latin, calypso, rock-a-billy, and rock and roll, lovingly curated by the Ventnor Exchange team.

Title: Sandbox: Arts Industry Event
Description: A residential inspiration & development programme for producers and creatives at Ventnor Fringe. Sandbox offers an opportunity for visiting professionals to fully immerse themselves in the festival over two days and three nights with a programme of talks, workshops, performances, secret shows, guided walks and communal meals. Sandbox has been designed to be more open ended than a training course and more fun than a conference! It's an opportunity to meet new people, be inspired, recharge and discover new work. Entry includes the cost of all activities and shows as part of the programme as well as a communal dinner on Thursday evening. The full programme will be announced shortly but the overall timings are as follows: Wednesday 22nd July 20:00hrs-23:00hrs Thursday 23rd July 09:30hrs - 22:00hrs (optional extra event to 00:00hrs) Friday 24th July 10:30hrs-20:30hrs (Followed by an optional Farewell Party to 00:00hrs) For additional details or advice on local accommodation/travel please contact us on hello@vfringe.co.uk

Title: RONNIE FONTAINE: CLOSE-UP AND CRIMINAL
Description: Close- Up and Criminal plunges you into the East End underworld of the 1980s. Ronnie “Flash” Fontaine, a former London gangster, unleashes thrilling tales of bank heists, armed robbery, failed jobs, and narrow escapes. A master hustler, he used his magic to con, smuggle, steal, and slip away from danger. Close-up magic performed inches from your eyes blurs the line between myth and reality. Edge-of-your-seat storytelling, jaw-dropping tricks, and dangerous charm make this intimate experience utterly unpredictable - pure dynamite. One performer. Ten at a table. Thirty unforgettable minutes.

Title: Andrew White: What a Life!
Description: An only-child with two siblings, Andrew White enjoyed a precocious childhood befriending retirees & penning self-indulgent poetry, until a literal circus clown warned him of imminent death on his 18th birthday… All the Jester foretold came to pass, and now Andrew must reflect on life without three close loved ones and do his best to honour their memories. Don’t miss this hilarious, honest & heartwarming new stand-up comedy show. ★★★★ “A plethora of memorable gags” (Chortle) “Very exciting & very funny” (Joe Lycett) “Outrageously funny” (Broadway World “Go see him, he’s sublime!” (Maisie Adam)

Title: Final Queen
Description: A Drag Circus Horror Comedy. Priscilla Queen of the Desert meets Texas Chainsaw Massacre under the Big Top! Join Australia’s own circus queen Grace Lightning as she embarks on a road trip through the outback, dodging deadly snakes and spiders, bigoted cryptids, and chainsaw-wielding bushrangers on a murderous rampage. But are these monsters really what they seem…? Expect haunted acrobatics, blood soaked hula hooping, and a whole lot of hair-raising hilarity, oozing with all your favourite horror tropes on the small stage.

Title: RONNIE FONTAINE: CLOSE-UP AND CRIMINAL
Description: Close- Up and Criminal plunges you into the East End underworld of the 1980s. Ronnie “Flash” Fontaine, a former London gangster, unleashes thrilling tales of bank heists, armed robbery, failed jobs, and narrow escapes. A master hustler, he used his magic to con, smuggle, steal, and slip away from danger. Close-up magic performed inches from your eyes blurs the line between myth and reality. Edge-of-your-seat storytelling, jaw-dropping tricks, and dangerous charm make this intimate experience utterly unpredictable - pure dynamite. One performer. Ten at a table. Thirty unforgettable minutes.

Title: Joe Kent-Walters is Frankie Monroe: DEAD!!! (Good Fun Time)
Description: Yorkshire’s biggest bastard and Edinburgh Comedy Award Best Newcomer 2024 is back, after yet another sold-out run at the Edinburgh Fringe Festival! This time he's dead… dead good! And also, dead. He's a dead man. Join the nation's favourite demonic entertainer, owner of a Working Mens Club in Rotherham what is also a portal to hell, as he dazzles you from beyond the grave. See him now before he is well and truly gone. "Plenty of fun for fans of Johnny Vegas, The Mighty Boosh and Vic and Bob" The Times ‘A devilishly funny show.’ ★★★★★ Telegraph

Title: TBC (totally brilliant conjuring)
Description: British Magic Champion Mark Shortland will blow your mind with some totally brilliant conjuring. This fun packed show will engage the audience with a mixture of illusion, audience participation and a "unique brand of comedy" which are all blended together with an individual style of magic and suggestion, creating an act the edge of your seat was made for. "You've blown our minds!" Ant and Dec "Very impressive, one of my favourite acts" Jonathan Ross "Your performance was brilliant, so funny and full of life" Teller

Title: Scaravelli Yoga Workshop - An Embodied Journey
Description: Embark on a transformational journey into the inner landscape of your body and breath with Scaravelli-inspired yoga teacher, aromatherapist, and healer Lew Williams. Through gently tending to and befriending your remarkable body—and following Lew’s uniquely intuitive, story-rich approach to yoga—you can discover a deep sense of strength, grace and freedom, no matter your age, flexibility, or level of experience.

Title: Fringe Frequencies: A Ventnor Sound Journey
Description: Step into a curated series of immersive Gong & Bowl Sound Baths inspired by the shifting atmosphere, coastline and natural rhythms of Ventnor. Each session carries its own distinct mood, frequencies and soundscape, connected through the creativity and energy Ventnor Fringe brings to the town each summer. Held beneath canvas in the park, expect resonant Sound, deep rest and the subtle backdrop of nature and festival life outside. Bring mats, pillows and blankets for comfort. Unsuitable for those with seizures, pacemakers or who are pregnant. Full details of contraindications on my website.

Title: Actor Workshop for Adults
Description: ACTOR WORKSHOP: VENTNOR FRINGE SPECIAL 90 Minute Fun Workshop with Emma Turner Come and play. This is 90 minutes of playful acting techniques, games, and exercises designed to help you explore and enjoy your acting capabilities. Whether you’ve never acted before or you’re a pro shaking off the cobwebs, this workshop is about fun, not perfection. What we’ll do: 1. Warm Up + Play — Ditch the day. Get your body, voice and imagination moving with games that wake you up. 2. Games + Exercises — Fast, fun techniques to unlock confidence, spontaneity, and that feeling of “oh, I can do that”. 3. Character + Scene Play — Try out scenes and improvise. Surprise yourself. No scripts to learn beforehand. 4. Take Home Tools — Simple ways to stay playful and present so you can keep exploring after the Fringe. Our vibe: Playful. Supportive. Creative. Safe. What happens in the room, stays in the room. No judgement. No pressure to be brilliant. Just space to try stuff and see what happens. This workshop is for you if: You’re curious about acting but never tried it. You used to act and want to play again. You’re an actor who needs to remember why you love it. You want to do something brave and fun at Ventnor Fringe. House rules: We start at 1pm sharp. Doors close 1:05pm — please be on time. Phones off. Be kind to yourself and each other. Be brave. It’s just play. What to bring: Water. Comfy clothes you can move in. Your sense of humour. That’s it. No experience needed. No pressure. Just play. Questions? DM @Actorworkshopiow on Instagram Emma Turner Actor + Acting Coach Spotlight: app.spotlight.com Instagram: @Actorworkshopiow Facebook: Actor Workshop “Come and play. See what you’re capable of.”

Title: On Holiday With Herzen
Description: A dryly humorous new play exploring Ventnor’s unexpected role in Russian history. On Holiday with Herzen revives characters from Turgenev’s Fathers and Sons, tracing a line from the Island to revolution, assassination, and the fate of modern Russia. A witty “postcard from Ventnor” imagining how history might have unfolded differently.

Title: Norvil & Josephine: Rabbits Out of the Hat
Description: After their 5-star studded season at Edinburgh Fringe, vaudevillian duo Norvil & Josephine present a magical extravaganza for all ages! Together they are a magician with a secret and an assistant with a mission in this whimsical comedy musical magic show, the likes of which the world has never seen. It's 1905 and life will never be the same again. It's time for the magician to chase new dreams while the assistant calls the shots. Expect breathtaking magic, sensational circus and showstopper songs as these Magic Circle magicians share a spectacularly silly and spellbinding story about being yourself no matter what. It's a heartwarming story within a whacky feel-good show. RABBITS OUT OF THE HAT appeals to both adult and family audiences in equal measure. The show has appeared in 85 theatre performances around the UK including 4 sold out seasons at The Well Walk Theatre in Hampstead and a run at The Pleasance King Dome at Edinburgh Festival Fringe.

Title: SILENT DISCO STRUT: A Wild Moving Disco Adventure Through Ventnor
Description: Silent Disco Strut. Wild, ridiculous and fun. A flamboyant, entertaining moving silent disco adventure through the fringe and Ventnor. It’s open to everyone! It’s open air and hysterical! Grab your headphones and hit the streets for a joy-fuelled, interactive silent disco like no other. Strut, dance and sing your way through Ventnor in a gloriously ridiculous roaming party led by a flamboyant host. Expect spontaneous dance-offs, shameless singalongs and plenty of playful mischief as you let go, unleash your inner child and become part of the spectacle."

Title: A Walk Through The Solar System
Description: Join Stokey Woodall, known as the Star-man, on an incredible solar system walk. This unique experience follows a 1,000-yard model, where each inch represents 100,000 miles in space Meet very day at 1400 hours, at the in Ventnor Exchange for a friendly celestial introduction chat .The walk will then commence from a scaled model of the Sun, conveniently located just meters away from the Exchange, outside the Mushi restaurant. and conclude at the downgraded planet Pluto at Ventnor Park

Title: A Haunted House

Title: Faery Tale Blues — Songs'n'Stories
Description: Following a rapidly sold out event at Ventnor Fringe last year ("Protest Days, Rebellion Nights"), storyteller Tony "Monty" Hirst and and folk/blues duo The AnyWhens (Glenn Koppany and Sue Cain) bring their new show "Faery Tale Blues" to the Bijou Tent for 2026. Featuring original and traditional songs, and traditional and historical tales, Monty + The AnyWhens invite you take a step outside the ordinary, to another world that is forever hidden in plain sight, to the lift the veil to the other world, the land of the Fae, and the time of Once.

Title: Rob Preston: Amazing Global Solutions (Work in Progress)
Description: Character comedian Rob Preston just wants everything to be perfect again. Join him and his unlikely cadre of alter-egos on their quest to dominate the content space, end mental health and bring lasting international peace to the entirety of the globe globally. A big ask? Sure. But someone's got to pull their finger out. BBC New Comedy Award and Pleasance Reserve Shortlist. Soon to appear in Leo Reich's Channel 4 and A24 sitcom It Gets Worse. Star of Fringe 2025 sell-out comedy play Brainsluts: **** (Guardian). **** (Times). **** (List). 'Crowd favourite' (BroadwayWorld.com)

Title: Burning Down the Horse
Description: The multi award-winning smash hit Burning Down the Horse comes to Ventnor! This immersive comedy epic drops you into the heart of the most iconic wooden animal in history - the Trojan Horse. Become part of this “infectiously funny” (Fringe Review) tale as you contend with heroic egos, class clashes and sword supply issues. There's only one rule: Odysseus' word is law. And for the love of gods - no naked flames! Created by the team behind last year’s smash It’s Gonna Blow!, join us and experience what really went on inside the belly of the beast.

Description: Explore Ventnor's famous Botanic Garden after hours as The Circus Trail returns with an all new cast of acrobats and performers. Once again transforming these tropical gardens in a unique promenade performance, created especially for Ventnor Fringe. The perfect festival adventure for adults and children of all ages.

Title: POLICE COPS
Description: POLICE COPS debut show; a globally-acclaimed comedy blockbuster that has enjoyed THREE sell out runs at London’s Soho Theatre and an OFFICIAL SELL OUT run at The Edinburgh Fringe Festival. The 7 time award winning production has delighted audiences around the world at The Melbourne International Comedy Festival, The Cape Town Fringe Festival and The National Arts Festival of South Africa. PRESENT DAY (of 1976). Straight-laced rookie Police Cop Jimmy Johnson is out to avenge his brother’s death, and he’s got to go it alone! (with his partner) Teamed up with a disgraced, retired renegade named Harrison, the pair begin to unearth the soily secrets that the case holds. POLICE COPS is a cinematic joyride, speeding down Adventure Avenue in a souped-up squad car. Oh, and did I mention...the steering wheel is made out of guns.

Title: Grapple
Description: An ageing man still trying to make sense of reality. Join me on my journey. Spoken word (poetry and Storytelling). For adult ears only.

Title: Fizzy
Description: Fizzy is a new, sharp, funny and heartfelt play about friendship, music and growing up in the late 1990s. Set in a world of mixtapes, gig queues and borrowed bravado, it follows a tight-knit group of friends building themselves out of mixtapes, in-jokes and stubborn hope, trying to work out who they are before the moment slips away. It’s loud, fast and funny, with something more fragile underneath: what happens when life suddenly becomes more complicated? For the mixtape-makers, the music lovers, the over-thinkers, and the ones who grew up a little out of step (or still working it out.)

Title: 100% Badgers with Matt Hobs
Description: 100% Badgers is the sell-out 2025 Edinburgh Fringe stand-up comedy show where Matt Hobs talks about how the worlds of humans and badgers collide. The show takes us on Hobs’ journey from a passing interest in badgers, to an obsession that leads to him standing in a field looking at a caged, frightened badger caught up in the misinformation of the bovine TB debate. We’ll learn about the European badger and its dachshund nemesis, the presidential American badger, the world-famous internet sensation that is the honey badger and many more. “Gripping” ★★★★.5, Edinburgh Reviews. “belly laughs” ★★★★.

Title: Lil Wenker: BOYKING (Work in Progress)
Description: ALL HAIL BOYKING! Eight-year-old Prince Wilfred (“Willy” for short) seeks power at all costs including but not limited to: death, destruction & dastardly deeds. Nowhere near in line for the throne, Prince Wilfred ruminates on a plan for total world domination while guzzling Mummy’s Milk. Bow down—this coronation’s a killer. Created by the award-winning performer-director duo Lil Wenker and Cecily Nash. Praise for past work (BANGTAIL): ★★★★★ “Sheer, brilliant, well executed madness” Three Weeks ​★★★★★ "A masterclass in clowning" Voice Mag

Title: Jess Robinson: Elton Reimagined
Description: Elton John's greatest hits are reimagined by multi award-winning Jess Robinson (Dead Ringers, The Last Leg), renowned for her pitch-perfect impressions. Celebrating Elton through the voices of iconic women: Britney, Barbra, Amy, and more. ***** (Edinburgh Festivals Magazine). ***** (Edinburgh Evening News). **** (Chortle.co.uk). 'It's like a mega concert with all your favourite stars' (ITV). 'Naturally funny, pitch perfect' (Telegraph). 'Slick impressions, super powered ballads' (Guardian). 'A star is born' (Metro).

Title: The Man in the Moon
Description: The Man in the Moon A guitar slinging Ventnor Taxi Drivers Tale Scribbled on a notepad in a hospital ward following a series of heart attacks, Simon Gooden was determined to tell one last story. This is it.

Title: Club Lunar
Description: Club Lunar is a Fringe institution in the making and the new beating heart of the festival. After a busy day watching shows make your way to Flowersbrook and discover secret circus, a cocktail bar, bands, DJs and a late-night cabaret every night. Each evening you'll find special guests headliners and an eclectic music policy spanning psych, reggae, swing, ska, jazz, African, Latin, calypso, rock-a-billy, and rock and roll, lovingly curated by the Ventnor Exchange team.

Title: Tarot Tales
Description: Delve deep into the wisdom and wonder of Tarot with this wildly imaginative live storytelling show. For hundreds of years, Tarot cards have fascinated and beguiled. They’ve told fortunes, uncovered secrets, and held up a dark mirror to the mysteries of life. Now performance storyteller Merl Fluin reveals the Major Arcana in a whole new light. Combining the Tarot’s symbolism with traditional tales from myth and folklore, she’ll lead you on a dance where every card is a portal to a glittering storyworld. Prepare to be spellbound.

Title: Jonathan Oldfield: Exquisite Corpse (Work in Progress)
Description: Award-winning character comedian Jonathan Oldfield is making his debut comedy hour. Creator and star of BBC Radio 4’s Time of the Week (winner of Best Sketch Show at British Comedy Guide Awards) and BBC New Comedy Awards Finalist. He’s also directed people like Lorna Rose Treen, Joe Kent-Walters, Alice Cockayne and Lucy Pearman. And you may have seen him in Star Wars: Andor or online in those sketches that someone sent to you. “Brilliant” The Observer “Left me snorting with laughter” BroadwayWorld “Always funny” Chortle

Title: Tom Ward: Post-millennium Tension
Description: Before Tom bails out on the modern world entirely to commit to nostalgia full-time, the burner phone using, Discman carrying, 1988-1995 obsessed freak is giving the present tense one last try. Not for him, but for his 2-year-old son who lives there. But how does 2026 compare to the one he grew up in? Tom explores the head f**k of popular culture in the modern world versus that of his youth in his trademark bittersweet style. As seen on Live at the Apollo, Roast Battle and Qi. “Fine jokes and star Presence” - Time Out “Seinfield-level observational comedy” - Chortle

Title: Tom Gamble
Description: Award-winning English guitarist Tom Gamble returns to the Isle of Wight for two special performances at the Ventnor Fringe Festival. Celebrating the release of his sixth solo album, “Six-String Vagabond”, join Tom at the Ventnor Arts Club for a genre-bending concert featuring original music, songs and virtuosic arrangements for guitar. Tom’s live performances have been praised for their “virtuosity, spontaneity and humour”. His recordings have been featured regularly on national radio, and his live tours have encompassed six continents and stages such as London’s Southbank Centre, Bridgewater

Title: Frankie Monroe's Late Night Pick N Mix
Description: Join Frankie Monro, the nation’s favourite demonic entertainer and Yorkshire's Biggest Bastard, as he presents a very special late night mixed bill featuring a host of some of the best clowns and surreal comedy of the festival. We're not quite sure what will happen, but one thing's for certain, It will be a fever dream you won't forget in a hurry. “Plenty of fun for fans of Johnny Vegas, The Mighty Boosh and Vic and Bob… but Kent-Walters’s mock-old-school-entertainer character has a charisma and a weirdness that is all his own too.” The Times

Title: Scaravelli Yoga Workshop - An Embodied Journey
Description: Embark on a transformational journey into the inner landscape of your body and breath with Scaravelli-inspired yoga teacher, aromatherapist, and healer Lew Williams. Through gently tending to and befriending your remarkable body—and following Lew’s uniquely intuitive, story-rich approach to yoga—you can discover a deep sense of strength, grace and freedom, no matter your age, flexibility, or level of experience.

Title: Fringe Frequencies: A Ventnor Sound Journey
Description: Step into a curated series of immersive Gong & Bowl Sound Baths inspired by the shifting atmosphere, coastline and natural rhythms of Ventnor. Each session carries its own distinct mood, frequencies and soundscape, connected through the creativity and energy Ventnor Fringe brings to the town each summer. Held beneath canvas in the park, expect resonant Sound, deep rest and the subtle backdrop of nature and festival life outside. Bring mats, pillows and blankets for comfort. Unsuitable for those with seizures, pacemakers or who are pregnant. Full details of contraindications on my website.

Title: Artist Breakfast hosted by Arts Council England
Description: Join us for an informal, social event with free pastries to meet fellow performers and creatives. Network, meet new people and discover more about development and support opportunities for artists in the region. Hosted by Arts Council England, who will be providing information about the funding and grant schemes they run. Artist Breakfast events are open to creatives, artists and students of any discipline, whether or not you're performing at Ventnor Fringe. Meet at the Fringe Village Box Office.

Title: Skysteamer to Athens: Steampunk Storytelling Extravaganza
Description: All aboard - the skysteamer is about to depart! Join C.J. Carter-Stephenson as he brings his steampunk novelette SKYSTEAMER TO ATHENS vividly to life. See a model airship soar over your head and meet a talking automaton puppet in this thrilling reader's theatre performance. A lavish sensory experience, which will transport you back to a fictionalised version of the 19th Century, where top hats and corsets are the height of fashion and the wonder of steam is transforming the world. Cast off your mooring ropes and prepare to soar into the clouds.

Title: Drawing Club: Keep Ventnor Weird
Description: Drawing Club is a social gathering bringing together all of those with a love of drawing, spanning all ages and abilities! These bi-weekly evenings at Ventnor Exchange are hosted by guest artist professional illustrator Katy Rose. In this taster session Katy will be on hand to set you some fun challenges and help offer advice and feedback on how to improve your technique. The theme to create a project together and also a take home keepsake is ‘Keep Ventnor Weird’. Children must be accompanied by a paying adult. All Materials provided.

Title: On Holiday With Herzen
Description: A dryly humorous new play exploring Ventnor’s unexpected role in Russian history. On Holiday with Herzen revives characters from Turgenev’s Fathers and Sons, tracing a line from the Island to revolution, assassination, and the fate of modern Russia. A witty “postcard from Ventnor” imagining how history might have unfolded differently.

Title: Norvil & Josephine: Rabbits Out of the Hat
Description: After their 5-star studded season at Edinburgh Fringe, vaudevillian duo Norvil & Josephine present a magical extravaganza for all ages! Together they are a magician with a secret and an assistant with a mission in this whimsical comedy musical magic show, the likes of which the world has never seen. It's 1905 and life will never be the same again. It's time for the magician to chase new dreams while the assistant calls the shots. Expect breathtaking magic, sensational circus and showstopper songs as these Magic Circle magicians share a spectacularly silly and spellbinding story about being yourself no matter what. It's a heartwarming story within a whacky feel-good show. RABBITS OUT OF THE HAT appeals to both adult and family audiences in equal measure. The show has appeared in 85 theatre performances around the UK including 4 sold out seasons at The Well Walk Theatre in Hampstead and a run at The Pleasance King Dome at Edinburgh Festival Fringe.

Title: SILENT DISCO STRUT: A Wild Moving Disco Adventure Through Ventnor
Description: Silent Disco Strut. Wild, ridiculous and fun. A flamboyant, entertaining moving silent disco adventure through the fringe and Ventnor. It’s open to everyone! It’s open air and hysterical! Grab your headphones and hit the streets for a joy-fuelled, interactive silent disco like no other. Strut, dance and sing your way through Ventnor in a gloriously ridiculous roaming party led by a flamboyant host. Expect spontaneous dance-offs, shameless singalongs and plenty of playful mischief as you let go, unleash your inner child and become part of the spectacle."

Title: A Walk Through The Solar System
Description: Join Stokey Woodall, known as the Star-man, on an incredible solar system walk. This unique experience follows a 1,000-yard model, where each inch represents 100,000 miles in space Meet very day at 1400 hours, at the in Ventnor Exchange for a friendly celestial introduction chat .The walk will then commence from a scaled model of the Sun, conveniently located just meters away from the Exchange, outside the Mushi restaurant. and conclude at the downgraded planet Pluto at Ventnor Park

Title: Marie Curie - Exposed
Description: Discover the remarkable story of Marie Curie in this faithful and evocative play that celebrates one of the most groundbreaking minds in history. Performed by Abbe Lee and Mark Stratford, the play takes us on the journey from Marie’s early struggles as a young immigrant scholar to her rise as a pioneering scientist. Through moments of love, humour, obsession, and triumph, the play reveals the sheer strength and heart behind the unstoppable genius who - with her husband Pierre - changed science and the world forever. Join us for this inspiring and intimate theatrical experience.

Title: A Haunted House
Description: Mime, clown and slapstick performer David Hoskin ('Phenomenal' ***** BroadwayWorld.com) presents A Haunted House; on a tour of a spooky old mansion, silly vignettes and strange time loops reveal murder, memory loss, and dancing zombies. It’s like spending the night with the League of Gentlemen and Jacques Tati in a Hammer horror film. In a dark, dark house on a dark, dark street, a house of horrors wakes from sleep... dare you enter? Mumbai Fringe '26 - Best International Comedy Show. ★★★★½ – EVERYTHING I LOVE about Fringe – Fringesider ★★★★ – Masterclass in physical theatre – BroadwayBaby

Title: SILENT DISCO STRUT: A Wild Moving Disco Adventure Through Ventnor
Description: Silent Disco Strut. Wild, ridiculous and fun. A flamboyant, entertaining moving silent disco adventure through the fringe and Ventnor. It’s open to everyone! It’s open air and hysterical! Grab your headphones and hit the streets for a joy-fuelled, interactive silent disco like no other. Strut, dance and sing your way through Ventnor in a gloriously ridiculous roaming party led by a flamboyant host. Expect spontaneous dance-offs, shameless singalongs and plenty of playful mischief as you let go, unleash your inner child and become part of the spectacle."

Title: Tetrapak Intaglio Printmaking workshop
Description: Come and join us for a special creative printmaking workshop led by Czech artist Petra Janouskova ART NOMAD on Tuesday 21st - Friday 24th, from 3 PM - 4:30PM. This workshop is for any experience level and ability—ages 12 and up. We will create a line-based design that will be transferred onto a Tetra Pak (a recycled milk or juice carton). The design will then be printed onto mixed-media paper using a printing press. This class will cover the full process of printmaking, from initial design through a finished print. Participants are encouraged to bring their own inspiration or choose from a variety of photographs provided by the class director.

Title: Faery Tale Blues — Songs'n'Stories
Description: Following a rapidly sold out event at Ventnor Fringe last year ("Protest Days, Rebellion Nights"), storyteller Tony "Monty" Hirst and and folk/blues duo The AnyWhens (Glenn Koppany and Sue Cain) bring their new show "Faery Tale Blues" to the Bijou Tent for 2026. Featuring original and traditional songs, and traditional and historical tales, Monty + The AnyWhens invite you take a step outside the ordinary, to another world that is forever hidden in plain sight, to the lift the veil to the other world, the land of the Fae, and the time of Once.

Title: Impromptu Shakespeare
Description: Hold onto your doublets, ruffs and trunk hose. The Bard is back! With story and verse improvised in the moment, enjoy a riotous new Shakespeare play every time – performed by Impromptu Shakespeare’s whip smart cast. Packed with priceless wit, lyrical smarts, romance, and a dash of double-crossing, this leading ensemble do the Bard proud with a brand new hit every time, inspired by audience suggestions. Returning for their seventh year at Ventnor Fringe, Impromptu Shakespeare have been a smash hit at the Edinburgh Fringe and Bristol Shakespeare Festival, and have appeared on BBC Shakespeare Live. WINNER: Best Comedy Event, Buxton Fringe 2021 “Shakespeare from the hip. Ingenious and very funny” – Will Gompertz, BBC Arts Editor “Simply sublime, side-splittingly hilarious improvised comedy almost worthy of the Bard himself” ★★★★★ Broadway Baby “Go and see this show. It’s intelligent, it’s funny, it’s all of the adjectives for brilliant. I’m still not sure how they did what they did” ★★★★★ Pocket Size Theatre “I would see it every night if I could” Voice Magazine “Anarchic and high-energy… Laughs galore” ★★★★★ The Reviews Hub “It will leave you hungry for more… See it as often as you can” Arts York “A joy… seemingly channelled from the playwright himself” ★★★★ North West End UK

Title: Lovestruck Alley
Description: Lovestruck Alley is an all-original musical, completely written, composed and produced by the young people of Carisbrooke Cast. Step back into the 1980's as we join our cast of misfits and slackers hiding behind the backdrop of a run-down bowling alley. Alli and Tash were settled into a life of mediocrity until the flamboyant Striker walks in and changes Alli’s life forever. Set to the soundtrack of new wave electro-pop, Lovestruck Alley explores the dynamics of friendship, loyalties, love and betrayal in the vein of classic eighties teen angst films like Say Anything and Sixteen Candles.

Title: Rob Preston: Amazing Global Solutions (Work in Progress)
Description: Character comedian Rob Preston just wants everything to be perfect again. Join him and his unlikely cadre of alter-egos on their quest to dominate the content space, end mental health and bring lasting international peace to the entirety of the globe globally. A big ask? Sure. But someone's got to pull their finger out. BBC New Comedy Award and Pleasance Reserve Shortlist. Soon to appear in Leo Reich's Channel 4 and A24 sitcom It Gets Worse. Star of Fringe 2025 sell-out comedy play Brainsluts: **** (Guardian). **** (Times). **** (List). 'Crowd favourite' (BroadwayWorld.com)

Title: Do All The Things
Description: From the award-winning idiots behind Game of Crones, Enter The Dragons and Witch Hunt, A&E Comedy bring you, Do All The Things. With a playful interactive twist this isn’t just a show, it’s an invitation to get involved, dose up on dopamine and soak up the serotonin. Part cabaret, part game show, part house party, Do All The Things is a celebration of commonality, an ode to the absurd, and, at its heart, a joyous middle finger to despair and disconnection. With big prizes.

Title: Circus Trail
Description: Explore Ventnor's famous Botanic Garden after hours as The Circus Trail returns with an all new cast of acrobats and performers. Once again transforming these tropical gardens in a unique promenade performance, created especially for Ventnor Fringe. The perfect festival adventure for adults and children of all ages.

Title: POLICE COPS
Description: POLICE COPS debut show; a globally-acclaimed comedy blockbuster that has enjoyed THREE sell out runs at London’s Soho Theatre and an OFFICIAL SELL OUT run at The Edinburgh Fringe Festival. The 7 time award winning production has delighted audiences around the world at The Melbourne International Comedy Festival, The Cape Town Fringe Festival and The National Arts Festival of South Africa. PRESENT DAY (of 1976). Straight-laced rookie Police Cop Jimmy Johnson is out to avenge his brother’s death, and he’s got to go it alone! (with his partner) Teamed up with a disgraced, retired renegade named Harrison, the pair begin to unearth the soily secrets that the case holds. POLICE COPS is a cinematic joyride, speeding down Adventure Avenue in a souped-up squad car. Oh, and did I mention...the steering wheel is made out of guns.

Title: Grapple
Description: An ageing man still trying to make sense of reality. Join me on my journey. Spoken word (poetry and Storytelling). For adult ears only.

Title: Fizzy
Description: Fizzy is a new, sharp, funny and heartfelt play about friendship, music and growing up in the late 1990s. Set in a world of mixtapes, gig queues and borrowed bravado, it follows a tight-knit group of friends building themselves out of mixtapes, in-jokes and stubborn hope, trying to work out who they are before the moment slips away. It’s loud, fast and funny, with something more fragile underneath: what happens when life suddenly becomes more complicated? For the mixtape-makers, the music lovers, the over-thinkers, and the ones who grew up a little out of step (or still working it out.)

Title: 100% Badgers with Matt Hobs
Description: 100% Badgers is the sell-out 2025 Edinburgh Fringe stand-up comedy show where Matt Hobs talks about how the worlds of humans and badgers collide. The show takes us on Hobs’ journey from a passing interest in badgers, to an obsession that leads to him standing in a field looking at a caged, frightened badger caught up in the misinformation of the bovine TB debate. We’ll learn about the European badger and its dachshund nemesis, the presidential American badger, the world-famous internet sensation that is the honey badger and many more. “Gripping” ★★★★.5, Edinburgh Reviews. “belly laughs” ★★★★.

Title: Tom Ballard: BE FUNNY CHALLENGE (IMPOSSIBLE) (Work in Progress)
Description: MELBOURNE COMEDY FESTIVAL & EDINBURGH COMEDY AWARD NOMINEE As seen on Fisk and Deadloch. As heard on Radio 4 and The Bugle. After a year away from touring, doing nothing but being nice and uncontroversial, Australia's Special Envoy For Comedy Tom Ballard returns in 2026 with a new show packed with jokes about war and being naughty and being kind and just how extremely normal everything is right now. You should definitely come along. What's the worst that could happen???? "One of the slickest hours of stand-up I've ever seen." Adam Hills

Title: Everything's been returned that was owed: A journey into Bob Dylan's roots
Description: Bob Dylan has been accused of stealing other people’s songs and tunes since 1961. He wrote 'yes, I am a thief of thoughts not, I pray, a stealer of souls / I have built an' rebuilt upon what is waiting. IN this concert Hugh Wright, presenter of two sell-out Dylan Fringe Shows gets together with David Jowitt (aka Antidepressance) to present a new show delving into the folk tradition which Dylan mined for ‘Blowin’ in the Wind and ‘Hard Rain’. The second half will be more 'rocky' and include friends such as Dick Taylor ('Pretty Things') and others from his recent 'Wild Cathedral' show

Title: Glenn Moore: Work In Progress
Description: Edinburgh Comedy Award Best Show nominee Glenn Moore (as seen on Live At The Apollo, Mock The Week, Have I Got News For You and Cats Does Countdown), one of the greatest joke writers of his generation, reads out a torrent of new, untested jokes from his notepads to see what works. Host of the hit comedy podcasts Budpod and Button Boys, and his own BBC Radio 4 series Glenn Moore's Almanac. ***** (Chortle.co.uk). ***** (Mail On Sunday).

Title: Club Lunar
Description: Club Lunar is a Fringe institution in the making and the new beating heart of the festival. After a busy day watching shows make your way to Flowersbrook and discover secret circus, a cocktail bar, bands, DJs and a late-night cabaret every night. Each evening you'll find special guests headliners and an eclectic music policy spanning psych, reggae, swing, ska, jazz, African, Latin, calypso, rock-a-billy, and rock and roll, lovingly curated by the Ventnor Exchange team.

Title: The Courtesan, the Prince, Three Lovers and a Poet
Description: A young woman in renaissance Venice makes a decision that echoes through the years. In Sheffield a young man receives news that will change the life of everyone he knows, and in a dark wood in Surrey a happily married couple find themselves in a very sticky situation. Tim Cooper, host of the Ventnor Exchange SpeakEasy and regular on the Solent spoken word scene, weaves from these stories a narrative about how we love and choose to be loved. Expect beautiful language, heartache and adult humour in a show you can still watch with the in-laws.

Title: The Night Baker
Description:
Welcome to the night shift. First time? You’ll be fine. Trust me. It’s like…it’s exactly like jet lag.” Written and performed by Giles Cooper, ‘The Night Baker’ takes you to the heart of the overnight shift at a bakery, exploring the physical and mental demands on the solitary baker. Shaping and baking sourdoughs, pastries, baguettes and more whilst navigating through an orchestra of oven alarms and other distractions, under the constant gaze of the all powerful wall clock. Journey deep into the early hours and beyond as the night baker faces the crushing pressure of getting everything baked to perfection, whilst being haunted by his own demons and memories, as an unidentified noise keeps getting closer and louder in the darkness outside. Giles Cooper is an actor who recently moved to the Isle of Wight. His stage work includes productions at the National Theatre, Shakespeares Globe, Manchester’s Royal Exchange and London’s West End. On screen he has appeared in films such as Pride and The Lady in The Van. Upcoming work includes the film adaptation of Prima Facie. As a baker, Giles has worked at Kossoffs and E5 Bakehouse in London, and currently works as the Head Baker for The Lost Mill Bakery in Cowes.

Being raised in some backwater town, he decides to leave his ivory tower and invest in a renovated warehouse loft in Peckham, looking for the exhilarating, gritty urban life. The life he finds though, is not the one he looks for. Fairly disappointed, he starts to educate his new friends and neighbors to make his world make sense again. A sharp, satirical, comedic theatre show on the choking reality of gentrification and the intellectual bewilderment of obscene wealth. Directed by award-winning director Katharina Boll.

Title: The Railwayman and Other Stories
Description: Join violinist and harp player Charlie Wild for her debut multimedia show "The Railwayman and Other Stories". With a backdrop of vintage 70's film footage from the Isle of Wight Railway, Charlie will present a musical retelling of her father's true tales and memories from the time he worked as a railwayman on the Isle of Wight in the 1970s and 80s. With beautiful original music composed and performed for each story, she promises you a heartwarming afternoon of new music, old stories, nostalgia, and humour.

Title: A Walk Through The Solar System
Description: Join Stokey Woodall, known as the Star-man, on an incredible solar system walk. This unique experience follows a 1,000-yard model, where each inch represents 100,000 miles in space Meet very day at 1400 hours, at the in Ventnor Exchange for a friendly celestial introduction chat .The walk will then commence from a scaled model of the Sun, conveniently located just meters away from the Exchange, outside the Mushi restaurant. and conclude at the downgraded planet Pluto at Ventnor Park

Title: The Queer Bash
Description: Performed originally for Isle of Wight Pride 2017 and UK Pride 2018, ‘The Queer Bash’ now returns for its third iteration. An original, devised play by Carl Burch for RedTIE Theatre. The Queer Bash is an in-depth exploration of the issues facing the LGBTQ+ community today and historical struggles that have never quite dwindled. It is also an unapologetic celebration of the LGBTQ+ community. A brutally honest and colourful emotional rollercoaster that is as funny as it is poignant.

Title: SILENT DISCO STRUT: A Wild Moving Disco Adventure Through Ventnor
Description: Silent Disco Strut. Wild, ridiculous and fun. A flamboyant, entertaining moving silent disco adventure through the fringe and Ventnor. It’s open to everyone! It’s open air and hysterical! Grab your headphones and hit the streets for a joy-fuelled, interactive silent disco like no other. Strut, dance and sing your way through Ventnor in a gloriously ridiculous roaming party led by a flamboyant host. Expect spontaneous dance-offs, shameless singalongs and plenty of playful mischief as you let go, unleash your inner child and become part of the spectacle."

Title: The Magic Juke Box
Description: 🎵 Press play… and let the magic begin! Step back in time for the ultimate feel-good magic show! Join magician Ian Vallance as he spins the jukebox back to the 70s and 80s, when he was a Butlins DJ and the dancefloor was always full. Now he brings those memories to life in a show where music and magic meet. From disco classics to pop anthems, every track sparks astonishing magic and illusion. Packed with nostalgia, laughter and surprises, The Magic Juke Box will leave you smiling all the way home.

Title: Confused Chronicles of Aleppo
Description: “A stunning piece of writing” – Reviewers Hub. An unusual love story during the Syrian civil war at beat rhythm. Gerard and Becky, two established photographers, travel in Turkey and Syria, crossing dramatic events such as the siege of Kobane and the battle of Aleppo. The most dramatic conflict of the third millennia read at beat rhythm through the eyes of two young adults ready to live and die for what they believe in. Confused Chronicles of Aleppo is a mixed reading / music performance.

Title: Impromptu Shakespeare
Description: Hold onto your doublets, ruffs and trunk hose. The Bard is back! With story and verse improvised in the moment, enjoy a riotous new Shakespeare play every time – performed by Impromptu Shakespeare’s whip smart cast. Packed with priceless wit, lyrical smarts, romance, and a dash of double-crossing, this leading ensemble do the Bard proud with a brand new hit every time, inspired by audience suggestions. Returning for their seventh year at Ventnor Fringe, Impromptu Shakespeare have been a smash hit at the Edinburgh Fringe and Bristol Shakespeare Festival, and have appeared on BBC Shakespeare Live. Description: Lovestruck Alley is an all-original musical, completely written, composed and produced by the young people of Carisbrooke Cast. Step back into the 1980's as we join our cast of misfits and slackers hiding behind the backdrop of a run-down bowling alley. Alli and Tash were settled into a life of mediocrity until the flamboyant Striker walks in and changes Alli’s life forever. Set to the soundtrack of new wave electro-pop, Lovestruck Alley explores the dynamics of friendship, loyalties, love and betrayal in the vein of classic eighties teen angst films like Say Anything and Sixteen Candles.

Title: Do All The Things
Description: From the award-winning idiots behind Game of Crones, Enter The Dragons and Witch Hunt, A&E Comedy bring you, Do All The Things. With a playful interactive twist this isn’t just a show, it’s an invitation to get involved, dose up on dopamine and soak up the serotonin. Part cabaret, part game show, part house party, Do All The Things is a celebration of commonality, an ode to the absurd, and, at its heart, a joyous middle finger to despair and disconnection. With big prizes.

Title: Garrett Millerick: We Tried it Your Way
Description: Creator of BBC Radio 4’s hit new sitcom Do Gooders returns with a unique live comedy experiment… Garrett’s built an AI of himself to see if it’s better at comedy than him. Maybe he can finally retire? Join Garrett and Garrett for a blistering hour of the no-holds-barred iconoclastic stand-up that has made him one of the stand out acts of his generation. 'A masterclass' ★★★★ Telegraph 'Wonderfully funny' ★★★★ Times 'Has the room erupting with laughter. Cathartic. Clever. Funny' ★★★★ Scotsman 'Devastatingly funny' ★★★★★ Chortle

Title: Drunk Women Solving Crime
Description: After four sell out years at V-Fringe, Ventnor's very own Hannah George is bringing her award-winning true crime comedy podcast to The Arena! With millions of downloads and listeners all over the world, Drunk Women Solving Crime is one of the top podcasts in the UK. Hannah, along with her co-detective Taylor Glenn, will 'solve' an Isle of Wight based true crime - so get your detective hat on and come and join us!

Title: The Fabled Isle
Description: Discover the Isle of Wight’s strange history through its stories, where fact and folklore weave seamlessly together. With an array of characters featuring sly smugglers, heroic hermits and raging river spirits, each tale holds at least a touch of truth - though exactly how much is for you to decide. Following sell-out shows in 2024 and 2025, Holly Medland returns to Ventnor Fringe with an enchanting retelling of the traditional stories that have shaped the Island and its people.

Title: The Quest of Ebondale
Description: The Quest of Ebondale is a tale of friendship, magic, and adventure. Join Artie and Hester as they escape their lonely existence in the real world to embark on a quest for the fairy queen's wand, in the magical kingdom of Ebondale. With their trusty guide on hand, the two evacuees venture into Ebondale’s Darkest Forest, meeting fairies, warlocks and other magical characters on the way. As all good quests do, they face challenges, riddles and dilemmas a plenty! Will they complete their quest and return the magic wand back to its rightful owner? A musical adventure for all ages!

Title: Lou Wall: Where Are All The Tall Grandmas (Work in Progress)
Description: Lou Wall is a multi-award-winning comedian, composer and content creator known for their genre-defying, politically savvy, and musically driven comedy. In 2023, Lou won the prestigious Moosehead Award and premiered Lou Wall vs The Internet at Melbourne Fringe and Edinburgh—going on to win Best of the Festival at the 2024 Sydney Comedy Festival and earning a nomination for Most Outstanding Show at the 2024 Melbourne Comedy Festival for their show "The Bisexual's Lament". In 2025, Breaking the Fifth Wall opened to critical acclaim with a Melbourne Comedy Festival Award nomination.

Title: Who are they?
Description: After having such a hoot at last year’s festival, Jack Campbell returns with a new show. Stemming from a flippant comment whilst watching local news on a Christmas visit back home a few years ago, Jack has had an idea for this show for 15 years. “Who are they?” is a historical, geographical, interactive and most importantly a pro-migration show in a day and age of increasing right-wing anti-immigration sentiment.

Title: Between the Lines: A Piano Recital by Francis Griffiths
Description: In this light and hopefully humorous piano recital, I will attempt to explore the evolution of music as it shifts from the expressive Romantic style of Chopin, through the impressionistic colours of Debussy and Satie, to the rhythmic freedom and improvisation of jazz. It will highlight how each era influenced the next, blurring the boundaries between classical tradition and modern musical expression.

Title: The Mind Reader Live
Description: Featuring one of the UK’s top hypnotists and renowned mind readers… Prepare for an evening of amazement and laughter as one of the UK’s top hypnotists and renowned mind-readers brings you an unmissable show in one incredible night. With multiple five star reviews, Daniel Sinclair has been described as “The next Derren Brown” (Phoenix Arts). “Blew me away” (Jimmy Carr). A master of combining suspense with comedy, music and truly jaw-dropping moments, Daniel’s shows will take you on a rollercoaster ride of your minds, leaving you laughing out loud while questioning your own realities. Show Trailer: https://youtu.be/q4ggW0V0Rpk A mind reading and mentalism show that will leave you on the edge of your seats. Expect West End quality entertainment, Derren Brown-style mind games and all-round psychological wonder as Daniel reads volunteers’ minds and makes astonishing predictions live on stage. About Daniel Daniel Sinclair is one of the UK’s leading comedy stage hypnotists, a renowned mind-reader and mentalist. He has been described as ‘the most impressive’ in his industry with performances spanning the globe from the West End to large theatres, festivals, TV and viral podcasts. Daniel has a knack for leaving crowds gobsmacked wherever he goes and has in recent years read the minds and hypnotised thousands of people.

Title: Club Lunar
Description: Club Lunar is a Fringe institution in the making and the new beating heart of the festival. After a busy day watching shows make your way to Flowersbrook and discover secret circus, a cocktail bar, bands, DJs and a late-night cabaret every night. Each evening you'll find special guests headliners and an eclectic music policy spanning psych, reggae, swing, ska, jazz, African, Latin, calypso, rock-a-billy, and rock and roll, lovingly curated by the Ventnor Exchange team.

Title: Rhymes Against Humanity
Description: Poetry and Spoken Word - but not as you know it. Ade Smith is a comedy performance poet. With his book (I Don't Ask For Much) already on the shelf and his second coming soon. Serving up his unique blend of honest, observational and comedy poetry, Ade shares his thoughts through the beauty of rhyme - on all the important issues in his life and yours! All he needs is a room full of people and a microphone. He doesn't ask for much!

Title: The Night Baker
Description:
Welcome to the night shift. First time? You’ll be fine. Trust me. It’s like…it’s exactly like jet lag.” Written and performed by Giles Cooper, ‘The Night Baker’ takes you to the heart of the overnight shift at a bakery, exploring the physical and mental demands on the solitary baker. Shaping and baking sourdoughs, pastries, baguettes and more whilst navigating through an orchestra of oven alarms and other distractions, under the constant gaze of the all powerful wall clock. Journey deep into the early hours and beyond as the night baker faces the crushing pressure of getting everything baked to perfection, whilst being haunted by his own demons and memories, as an unidentified noise keeps getting closer and louder in the darkness outside. Giles Cooper is an actor who recently moved to the Isle of Wight. His stage work includes productions at the National Theatre, Shakespeares Globe, Manchester’s Royal Exchange and London’s West End. On screen he has appeared in films such as Pride and The Lady in The Van. Upcoming work includes the film adaptation of Prima Facie. As a baker, Giles has worked at Kossoffs and E5 Bakehouse in London, and currently works as the Head Baker for The Lost Mill Bakery in Cowes.

Being raised in some backwater town, he decides to leave his ivory tower and invest in a renovated warehouse loft in Peckham, looking for the exhilarating, gritty urban life. The life he finds though, is not the one he looks for. Fairly disappointed, he starts to educate his new friends and neighbors to make his world make sense again. A sharp, satirical, comedic theatre show on the choking reality of gentrification and the intellectual bewilderment of obscene wealth. Directed by award-winning director Katharina Boll.
The Fringe Village Box Office	2026-07-26	13:00	14:00	SILENT DISCO STRUT: A Wild Moving Disco Adventure Through Ventnor	https://purchase.vfringe.co.uk/EventAvailability?EventId=58201		Spoken Word, Walks & Talks	true	false	Silent Disco Strut. Wild, ridiculous and fun. A flamboyant, entertaining moving silent disco adventure through the fringe and Ventnor. It’s open to everyone! It’s open air and hysterical! Grab your headphones and hit the streets for a joy-fuelled, interactive silent disco like no other. Strut, dance and sing your way through Ventnor in a gloriously ridiculous roaming party led by a flamboyant host. Expect spontaneous dance-offs, shameless singalongs and plenty of playful mischief as you let go, unleash your inner child and become part of the spectacle.

Title: The Railwayman and Other Stories
Description: Join violinist and harp player Charlie Wild for her debut multimedia show "The Railwayman and Other Stories". With a backdrop of vintage 70's film footage from the Isle of Wight Railway, Charlie will present a musical retelling of her father's true tales and memories from the time he worked as a railwayman on the Isle of Wight in the 1970s and 80s. With beautiful original music composed and performed for each story, she promises you a heartwarming afternoon of new music, old stories, nostalgia, and humour.

Title: A Walk Through The Solar System
Description: Join Stokey Woodall, known as the Star-man, on an incredible solar system walk. This unique experience follows a 1,000-yard model, where each inch represents 100,000 miles in space Meet very day at 1400 hours, at the in Ventnor Exchange for a friendly celestial introduction chat .The walk will then commence from a scaled model of the Sun, conveniently located just meters away from the Exchange, outside the Mushi restaurant. and conclude at the downgraded planet Pluto at Ventnor Park

Title: Aspire Creative Hub Presents...
Description: Aspire Creative Hub Presents... Acts from our Community Music Facilities. Freestyle Rap, Rock and Pop Youth Bands, and Loads of Fabulous Live Music from our Community Groups. Come and see what The Aspire Creative Hub is all about! Based in Ryde, and now expanding to town and venues across the Island, we provide accessible music workshops and live performance opportunities for everyone, from Youth Music Clubs, for young people aged from 8 years old and up, to our Re:Connect band formed to reconnect older Musicians and those living with Dementia with their love of music... Adult Music Groups supporting mental health through creativity to our Community Choir Group. This is our 7th year at Ventnor Fringe and we are looking forward to showcasing an exciting range of musical acts that use our facilities.

Title: SILENT DISCO STRUT: A Wild Moving Disco Adventure Through Ventnor
Description: Silent Disco Strut. Wild, ridiculous and fun. A flamboyant, entertaining moving silent disco adventure through the fringe and Ventnor. It’s open to everyone! It’s open air and hysterical! Grab your headphones and hit the streets for a joy-fuelled, interactive silent disco like no other. Strut, dance and sing your way through Ventnor in a gloriously ridiculous roaming party led by a flamboyant host. Expect spontaneous dance-offs, shameless singalongs and plenty of playful mischief as you let go, unleash your inner child and become part of the spectacle."

Title: The Magic Juke Box
Description: 🎵 Press play… and let the magic begin! Step back in time for the ultimate feel-good magic show! Join magician Ian Vallance as he spins the jukebox back to the 70s and 80s, when he was a Butlins DJ and the dancefloor was always full. Now he brings those memories to life in a show where music and magic meet. From disco classics to pop anthems, every track sparks astonishing magic and illusion. Packed with nostalgia, laughter and surprises, The Magic Juke Box will leave you smiling all the way home.

Title: Confused Chronicles of Aleppo
Description: “A stunning piece of writing” – Reviewers Hub. An unusual love story during the Syrian civil war at beat rhythm. Gerard and Becky, two established photographers, travel in Turkey and Syria, crossing dramatic events such as the siege of Kobane and the battle of Aleppo. The most dramatic conflict of the third millennia read at beat rhythm through the eyes of two young adults ready to live and die for what they believe in. Confused Chronicles of Aleppo is a mixed reading / music performance.

Title: Do All The Things

Title: Thor Stenhaug (Work in Progress)
Description: Channel 4 Sean Lock Award Finalist Thor Stenhaug is a Norwegian comedian based in London. He had a completely sold-out run at the Edinburgh Fringe 2025 with his debut show "One Night Stand Baby". Thor has headlined top comedy clubs across the country, and recently completed a run at Soho Theatre, London. Thor has offered tour support for Vittorio Angelone, Fern Brady, Josh Widdecombe, Larry Dean and Jack Dee, and was shortlisted for British Comedian of the Year - despite not being British (wonder what happened there.) ★★★★ The Telegraph ★★★★ The Scotsman

Title: The Hypnotist Live
Description: Featuring one of the UK’s top hypnotists… Prepare for an evening of amazement and laughter as one of the UK’s top hypnotists brings you an unmissable show in one incredible night. A master of combining suspense with comedy, music and truly jaw-dropping moments, Daniel’s show will take you on a rollercoaster ride of your minds, leaving you laughing out loud while questioning your own realities. Show Trailer: https://youtu.be/NIYeTZl47TM A mesmerisingly hilarious hypnosis show where volunteers from the audience have their imaginations unlocked and realities altered, all through the power of hypnosis. Buckle up tight. Prepare to be spellbound and in fits of laughter. Choose to SEE the show or BE the show! About Daniel Daniel Sinclair is one of the UK’s leading comedy stage hypnotists, a renowned mind-reader and mentalist. He has been described as ‘the most impressive’ in his industry with performances spanning the globe from the West End to large theatres, festivals, TV and viral podcasts. Title: Rosie Holt: The Illegal Aliens have landed! (Work in Progress)
Description: Rosie Holt and her MP alter ego are back with a new show, this time fighting their biggest foe yet. While battling bad polls and the rise of the far right, Labour suddenly must contend with an actual alien invasion. A Sci-Fi streaked satire. “Can Holt’s comedy career survive the advent of a Labour government? On this evidence, you’d fancy her chances.” - The Guardian. “Tightly crafted and brutal political satire with Orwellian flourishes” - The Evening Standard.

Title: Scratch Night at Ventnor Fringe
Description: Join us for Scratch Night - a lively evening of short, punchy performances and works-in-progress from Island artists. Presented by IW Creative Network & Mayflower, Scratch Night showcases brand-new theatre, comedy, spoken word and performance pieces currently in development. From experimental theatre to musical comedy, each act offers a glimpse of new work being tested in front of a live audience. Scratch Nights are all about experimentation. Expect a relaxed and supportive atmosphere where audiences can enjoy fresh ideas and discover emerging work.
